It's assigned to one of the executors for processing.\n    50→ * 2. It's locked for committing, removal, other manipulations etc.\n    51→ * 3. Neither 1 or 2, i.e. it stays idle. This is possible if we do not have enough executors or because those tasks\n    52→ *    are not processable (e.g. because no records fetched) yet.\n    53→ */\n    54→public final class DefaultTaskManager implements TaskManager {\n    55→\n    56→    private final Time time;\n    57→    private final Logger log;\n    58→    private final TasksRegistry tasks;\n    59→\n    60→    private final Lock tasksLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    61→    private final Condition tasksCondition = tasksLock.newCondition();\n    62→    private final List<TaskId> lockedTasks = new ArrayList<>();\n    63→    private final Map<TaskId, StreamsException> uncaughtExceptions = new HashMap<>();\n    64→    private final Map<TaskId, TaskExecutor> assignedTasks = new HashMap<>();\n    65→    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    66→\n    67→    private final List<TaskExecutor> taskExecutors;\n    68→\n    69→    public static class DefaultTaskExecutorCreator implements TaskExecutorCreator {\n    70→        @Override\n    71→        public TaskExecutor create(final TaskManager taskManager, final String name, final Time time, final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n    72→            return new DefaultTaskExecutor(taskManager, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata);\n    73→        }\n    74→    }\n    75→\n    76→    public DefaultTaskManager(final Time time,\n    77→                              final String clientId,\n    78→                              final TasksRegistry tasks,\n    79→                              final TaskExecutorCreator executorCreator,\n    80→                              final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata,\n    81→                              final int numExecutors\n    82→                              ) {\n    83→        final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", clientId);\n    84→        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n    85→        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskManager.class);\n    86→        this.time = time;\n    87→        this.tasks = tasks;\n    88→        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n    89→\n    90→        this.taskExecutors = new ArrayList<>(numExecutors);\n    91→        for (int i = 1; i <= numExecutors; i++) {\n    92→            final String name = clientId + \"-TaskExecutor-\" + i;\n    93→            this.taskExecutors.add(executorCreator.create(this, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata));\n    94→        }\n    95→    }\n    96→\n    97→    @Override\n    98→    public StreamTask assignNextTask(final TaskExecutor executor) {\n    99→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   100→            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n   101→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for getting next task to assign is unrecognized\");\n   102→            }\n   103→\n   104→            // the most naive scheduling algorithm for now: give the next unlocked, unassigned, and  processable task\n   105→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   106→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   107→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   108→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   109→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   110→                ) {\n   111→\n   112→                    assignedTasks.put(task.id(), executor);\n   113→\n   114→                    log.debug(\"Assigned task {} to executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   115→\n   116→                    return task;\n   117→                }\n   118→            }\n   119→\n   120→            log.debug(\"Found no assignable task for executor {}\", executor.name());\n   121→\n   122→            return null;\n   123→        });\n   124→    }\n   125→\n   126→    @Override\n   127→    public void awaitProcessableTasks(final Supplier<Boolean> isShuttingDown) throws InterruptedException {\n   128→        final boolean interrupted = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   129→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   130→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   131→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   132→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   133→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   134→                ) {\n   135→                    log.debug(\"Await unblocked: returning early from await since a processable task {} was found\", task.id());\n   136→                    return false;\n   137→                }\n   138→            }\n   139→            try {\n   140→                // We re-check the shutdownRequest atomic boolean to avoid a race condition. If this thread was\n   141→                // previously interrupted while awaiting tasksCondition, it is possible to miss the signalAll that\n   142→                // is called during shutdown. If this happens, we end up blocking in the await forever.\n   143→                if (!isShuttingDown.get()) {\n   144→                    log.debug(\"Await blocking\");\n   145→                    tasksCondition.await();\n   146→                } else {\n   147→                    log.debug(\"Not awaiting since shutdown was requested\");\n   148→                }\n   149→            } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n   150→                // we interrupt the thread for shut down and pause.\n   151→                // we can ignore this exception.\n   152→                log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Interrupted while waiting for processable tasks\");\n   153→                return true;\n   154→            }\n   155→            log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Woken up to check for processable tasks\");\n   156→            return false;\n   157→        });\n   158→\n   159→        if (interrupted) {\n   160→            throw new InterruptedException();\n   161→        }\n   162→    }\n   163→\n   164→    public void signalTaskExecutors() {\n   165→        log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   166→        executeWithTasksLocked(tasksCondition::signalAll);\n   167→    }\n   168→\n   169→    @Override\n   170→    public void unassignTask(final StreamTask task, final TaskExecutor executor) {\n   171→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   172→            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n   173→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for unassign task is unrecognized\");\n   174→            }\n   175→\n   176→            final TaskExecutor lockedExecutor = assignedTasks.get(task.id());\n   177→            if (lockedExecutor == null || lockedExecutor != executor) {\n   178→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Task \" + task.id() + \" is not locked by the executor\");\n   179→            }\n   180→\n   181→            assignedTasks.remove(task.id());\n   182→\n   183→            log.debug(\"Unassigned {} from executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   184→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   185→        });\n   186→    }\n   187→\n   188→    @Override\n   189→    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   190→        final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> result = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n   191→\n   192→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   193→            result.complete(null);\n   194→            return result;\n   195→        }\n   196→\n   197→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   198→            lockedTasks.addAll(taskIds);\n   199→\n   200→            final Set<TaskId> remainingTaskIds = new ConcurrentSkipListSet<>(taskIds);\n   201→\n   202→            for (final TaskId taskId : taskIds) {\n   203→                final Task task = tasks.initializedTask(taskId);\n   204→\n   205→                if (task == null) {\n   206→                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Trying to lock task \" + taskId + \" but it's not owned\");\n   207→                }\n   208→\n   209→                if (!task.isActive()) {\n   210→                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The locking task \" + taskId + \" is not an active task\");\n   211→                }\n   212→\n   213→                if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   214→                    final TaskExecutor executor = assignedTasks.get(taskId);\n   215→                    log.debug(\"Requesting release of task {} from {}\", taskId, executor.name());\n   216→                    final KafkaFuture<StreamTask> future = executor.unassign();\n   217→                    future.whenComplete((streamTask, throwable) -> {\n   218→                        if (throwable != null) {\n   219→                            result.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n   220→                        } else {\n   221→                            assert !assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId);\n   222→                            // It can happen that the executor handed back the task before we asked it to\n   223→                            // in which case `streamTask` will be null here.\n   224→                            assert streamTask == null || streamTask.id() == taskId;\n   225→                            remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   226→                            if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   227→                                result.complete(null);\n   228→                            }\n   229→                        }\n   230→                    });\n   231→                } else {\n   232→                    remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   233→                    if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   234→                        result.complete(null);\n   235→                    }\n   236→                }\n   237→            }\n   238→\n   239→            return result;\n   240→        });\n   241→    }\n   242→\n   243→    @Override\n   244→    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockAllTasks() {\n   245→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() ->\n   246→            lockTasks(tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(Task::id).collect(Collectors.toSet()))\n   247→        );\n   248→    }\n   249→\n   250→    @Override\n   251→    public void unlockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   252→\n   253→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   254→            return;\n   255→        }\n   256→\n   257→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   258→            lockedTasks.removeAll(taskIds);\n   259→            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   260→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   261→        });\n   262→    }\n   263→\n   264→    @Override\n   265→    public void unlockAllTasks() {\n   266→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> unlockTasks(tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(Task::id).collect(Collectors.toSet())));\n   267→    }\n   268→\n   269→    @Override\n   270→    public void add(final Set<StreamTask> tasksToAdd) {\n   271→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   272→            for (final StreamTask task : tasksToAdd) {\n   273→                tasks.addActiveTask(task);\n   274→            }\n   275→            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   276→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   277→        });\n   278→\n   279→        log.info(\"Added tasks {} to the task manager to process\", tasksToAdd);\n   280→    }\n   281→\n   282→    @Override\n   283→    public void remove(final TaskId taskId) {\n   284→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   285→            if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   286→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is still assigned to executors\");\n   287→            }\n   288→\n   289→            if (!lockedTasks.contains(taskId)) {\n   290→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not locked yet by the task manager\");\n   291→            }\n   292→\n   293→            if (!tasks.containsInitialized(taskId)) {\n   294→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not owned by the task manager\");\n   295→            }\n   296→\n   297→            tasks.removeTask(tasks.initializedTask(taskId));\n   298→        });\n   299→\n   300→        log.info(\"Removed task {} from the task manager\", taskId);\n   301→    }\n   302→\n   303→    @Override\n   304→    public Set<ReadOnlyTask> getTasks() {\n   305→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(ReadOnlyTask::new).collect(Collectors.toSet()));\n   306→    }\n   307→\n   308→    @Override\n   309→    public void setUncaughtException(final StreamsException exception, final TaskId taskId) {\n   310→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   311→\n   312→            if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   313→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"An uncaught exception can only be set as long as the task is still assigned\");\n   314→            }\n   315→\n   316→            if (uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   317→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The uncaught exception must be cleared before restarting processing\");\n   318→            }\n   319→\n   320→            uncaughtExceptions.put(taskId, exception);\n   321→        });\n   322→\n   323→        log.info(\"Set an uncaught exception of type {} for task {}, with error message: {}\",\n   324→            exception.getClass().getName(),\n   325→            taskId,\n   326→            exception.getMessage());\n   327→    }\n   328→\n   329→    public Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> drainUncaughtExceptions() {\n   330→        final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> returnValue = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   331→            final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> result = new HashMap<>(uncaughtExceptions);\n   332→            uncaughtExceptions.clear();\n   333→            return result;\n   334→        });\n   335→\n   336→        if (!returnValue.isEmpty()) {\n   337→            log.debug(\"Drained {} uncaught exceptions\", returnValue.size());\n   338→        }\n   339→\n   340→        return returnValue;\n   341→    }\n   342→\n   343→    public boolean hasUncaughtException(final TaskId taskId) {\n   344→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId));\n   345→    }\n   346→\n   347→    private void executeWithTasksLocked(final Runnable action) {\n   348→        tasksLock.lock();\n   349→        try {\n   350→            action.run();\n   351→        } finally {\n   352→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   353→        }\n   354→    }\n   355→\n   356→    private <T> T returnWithTasksLocked(final Supplier<T> action) {\n   357→        tasksLock.lock();\n   358→        try {\n   359→            return action.get();\n   360→        } finally {\n   361→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   362→        }\n   363→    }\n   364→\n   365→    private boolean canProgress(final StreamTask task, final long nowMs) {\n   366→        return\n   367→            taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(task, nowMs) && task.isProcessable(nowMs) ||\n   368→                taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(task) && (task.canPunctuateStreamTime() || task.canPunctuateSystemTime());\n   369→    }\n   370→\n   371→    public void startTaskExecutors() {\n   372→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n   373→            t.start();\n   374→        }\n   375→    }\n   376→\n   377→    public void shutdown(final Duration duration) {\n   378→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n   379→            t.requestShutdown();\n   380→        }\n   381→        signalTaskExecutors();\n   382→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n   383→            t.awaitShutdown(duration);\n   384→        }\n   385→    }\n   386→}\n static org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.StateManagerUtil.parseTaskDirectoryName;\n    82→\n    83→/**\n    84→ * Manages the directories where the state of Tasks owned by a {@link private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(StateDirectory.class);\n    93→    static final String LOCK_FILE_NAME = \".lock\";\n    94→\n    95→    /* The process file is used to persist \"kafka-streams-process-metadata\";\n    99→\n   100→    @JsonIgnoreProperties(ignoreUnknown = true)\n   101→    static class StateDirectoryProcessFile {\n   102→        @JsonProperty\n   103→        private final UUID processId;\n   104→\n   105→        // required by jackson -- do not remove, your IDE may be warning that this is unused but it's lying to you\n   106→        public = processId;\n   112→        }\n   113→    }\n   114→\n   115→    private final Object taskDirCreationLock = new Object();\n   116→    private final Time time;\n   117→    private final String ConcurrentMap<TaskId, Thread> lockedTasksToOwner = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\n   123→\n   124→    private FileChannel stateDirLockChannel;\n   125→    private FileLock stateDirLock;\n   126→\n   127→    private final StreamsConfig config;\n   128→    private final Set<TaskId> tasksInLocalState = new ConcurrentSkipListSet<>();\n   129→    private final Map<TaskId, Long> taskOffsetSums = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\n   130→\n   131→    /**\n   132→     * Ensures that the state base directory as well as the application's sub-directory are created.\n   133→     *\n   134→     * @param config              streams application configuration to read the root state directory path\n   135→     * @param time                system timer used to execute periodic cleanup procedure\n   136→     * @param hasPersistentStores only when the application's topology does have stores persisted on local file\n   137→     *                            system, we would go ahead and auto-create the corresponding application / task / store\n   138→     *                            directories whenever necessary; otherwise no directories would be created.\n   139→     * @param hasNamedTopologies  whether this application is composed of independent named topologies\n   140→     *\n   141→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the base state directory or application state directory does not exist\n   142→     *                                 and could not be created when hasPersistentStores is enabled.\n   143→     */\n   144→    public StateDirectory(final StreamsConfig config, final Time time, final boolean hasPersistentStores, final boolean hasNamedTopologies) {\n   145→        this.time = time;\n   146→        this.hasPersistentStores = hasPersistentStores;\n   147→        this.hasNamedTopologies = hasNamedTopologies;\n   148→        this.appId = config.getString(StreamsConfig.APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG);\n   149→        this.config = config;\n   150→        final String stateDirName = config.getString(StreamsConfig.STATE_DIR_CONFIG);\n   151→        final File baseDir = new File(stateDirName);\n   152→        stateDir = new File(baseDir, appId);\n   153→\n   154→        if (this.hasPersistentStores) {\n   155→            if (!baseDir.exists() && !baseDir.mkdirs()) {\n   156→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   157→                    String.format(\"base state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", stateDirName));\n   158→            }\n   159→            if (!stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.mkdir()) {\n   160→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   161→                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", stateDir.getPath()));\n   162→            } else if (stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.isDirectory()) {\n   163→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   164→                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", stateDir.getPath()));\n   165→            }\n   166→\n   167→            if (stateDirName.startsWith(System.getProperty(\"java.io.tmpdir\"))) {\n   168→                log.warn(\"Using an OS temp directory in the state.dir property can cause failures with writing\" +\n   169→                    \" the checkpoint file due to the fact that this directory can be cleared by the OS.\" +\n   170→                    \" Resolved state.dir: [\" + stateDirName + \"]\");\n   171→\n   172→            }\n   173→            // change the dir permission to \"rwxr-x---\" to avoid world readable\n   174→            configurePermissions(baseDir);\n   175→            configurePermissions(stateDir);\n   176→        }\n   177→    }\n   178→\n   179→    private void configurePermissions(final File file) {\n   180→        final Path path = file.toPath();\n   181→        if (path.getFileSystem().supportedFileAttributeViews().contains(\"posix\")) {\n   182→            final Set<PosixFilePermission> perms = EnumSet.of(\n   183→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_READ,\n   184→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_WRITE,\n   185→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_EXECUTE,\n   186→                    PosixFilePermission.GROUP_READ,\n   187→                    PosixFilePermission.GROUP_EXECUTE\n   188→            );\n   189→            if (config.getBoolean(StreamsConfig.ALLOW_OS_GROUP_WRITE_ACCESS_CONFIG)) {\n   190→                perms.add(PosixFilePermission.GROUP_WRITE);\n   191→            }\n   192→            try {\n   193→                Files.setPosixFilePermissions(path, perms);\n   194→            } catch (final IOException e) {\n   195→                log.error(\"Error changing permissions for the directory {} \", path, e);\n   196→            }\n   197→        } else {\n   198→            boolean set = file.setReadable(true, true);\n   199→            set &= file.setWritable(true, true);\n   200→            set &= file.setExecutable(true, true);\n   201→            if (!set) {\n   202→                log.error(\"Failed to change permissions for the directory {}\", file);\n   203→            }\n   204→        }\n   205→    }\n   206→\n   207→    /**\n   208→     * @return true if the state directory was successfully locked\n   209→     */\n   210→    private boolean lockStateDirectory() {\n   211→        final File lockFile = new File(stateDir, LOCK_FILE_NAME);\n   212→        try {\n   213→            stateDirLockChannel = FileChannel.open(lockFile.toPath(), StandardOpenOption.CREATE, StandardOpenOption.WRITE);\n   214→            stateDirLock = tryLock(stateDirLockChannel);\n   215→        } catch (final IOException e) {\n   216→            log.error(\"Unable to lock the state directory due to unexpected exception\", e);\n   217→            throw new ProcessorStateException(String.format(\"Failed to lock the state directory [%s] during startup\",\n   218→                stateDir.getAbsolutePath()), e);\n   219→        }\n   220→        return stateDirLock != null;\n   221→    }\n   222→\n   223→    /**\n   224→     * @throws LockException If another process already locks any of StateDirectory\n   225→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if any of task directory does not exist and could not be created\n   226→    */\n   227→    public void initializeStartupStores(final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata,\n   228→                                        final LogContext logContext,\n   229→                                        final StreamsMetricsImpl metricsImpl) {\n   230→        final List<TaskDirectory> nonEmptyTaskDirectories = listNonEmptyTaskDirectories();\n   231→        if (hasPersistentStores && !nonEmptyTaskDirectories.isEmpty()) {\n   232→            final boolean eosEnabled = StreamsConfigUtils.eosEnabled(config);\n   233→\n   234→            // Initialize thread-specific resources needed to open stores in the state directory\n   235→            final String threadLogPrefix = String.format(\"[%s]\", Thread.currentThread().getName());\n   236→            final ThreadCache dummyCache = new ThreadCache(new LogContext(threadLogPrefix), 0L, metricsImpl);\n   237→\n   238→            // discover all non-empty task directories in StateDirectory\n   239→            for (final TaskDirectory taskDirectory : nonEmptyTaskDirectories) {\n   240→                final String dirName = taskDirectory.file().getName();\n   241→                final TaskId task = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDirectory.namedTopology());\n   242→                final ProcessorTopology subTopology = topologyMetadata.buildSubtopology(task);\n   243→\n   244→                // we still check if the task's sub-topology is stateful, even though we know its directory contains state,\n   245→                // because it's possible that the topology has changed since that data was written, and is now stateless\n   246→                // this therefore prevents us from creating unnecessary stores just because of some left-over state\n   247→                if (subTopology.hasStateWithChangelogs()) {\n   248→                    final Set<TopicPartition> inputPartitions = topologyMetadata.nodeToSourceTopics(task).values().stream()\n   249→                            .flatMap(Collection::stream)\n   250→                            .map(t -> new TopicPartition(t, task.partition()))\n   251→                            .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n   252→                    // Open a temporary state manager that will open the stores inside the subtopology\n   253→                    final ProcessorStateManager temporaryStateManager = ProcessorStateManager.createStartupTaskStateManager(\n   254→                        task,\n   255→                        eosEnabled,\n   256→                        logContext,\n   257→                        this,\n   258→                        subTopology.storeToChangelogTopic(),\n   259→                        inputPartitions\n   260→                    );\n   261→\n   262→                    final StartupContext initContext = new StartupContext(task, config, temporaryStateManager, metricsImpl, dummyCache);\n   263→                    try {\n   264→                        // We only handle TaskCorruptedException at this point. Any other exception is considered fatal.\n   265→                        StateManagerUtil.registerStateStores(log, threadLogPrefix, subTopology, temporaryStateManager, this, initContext);\n   266→                        temporaryStateManager.checkpoint();\n   267→                    } catch (final TaskCorruptedException tce) {\n   268→                        // At this point, we only log a warning and continue with the startup store initialization.\n   269→                        // The task-corrupted exception will be handled in the first Task assignment phase.\n   270→                        log.warn(\"Failed to register startup state stores for task {}: {}\", task, tce.getMessage());\n   271→                    } finally {\n   272→                        // Make sure the state manager writes the local checkpoint file before closing the stores\n   273→                        // This will be replaced in the future when removing the checkpoint file dependency.\n   274→                        temporaryStateManager.close();\n   275→                    }\n   276→                    tasksInLocalState.add(task);\n   277→                }\n   278→            }\n   279→        }\n   280→    }\n   281→\n   282→    public boolean hasStartupTasks() {\n   283→        return !tasksInLocalState.isEmpty();\n   284→    }\n   285→\n   286→    public synchronized boolean removeStartupState(final TaskId taskId) {\n   287→        final boolean removed = tasksInLocalState.remove(taskId);\n   288→        if (removed) {\n   289→            lockedTasksToOwner.put(taskId, Thread.currentThread());\n   290→        }\n   291→        return removed;\n   292→    }\n   293→\n   294→\n   295→    private void unlockStartupStores() {\n   296→        for (final TaskId task : tasksInLocalState) {\n   297→            unlock(task);\n   298→        }\n   299→    }\n   300→\n   301→    public Map<TaskId, Long> taskOffsetSums(final Set<TaskId> tasks) {\n   302→        return taskOffsetSums.entrySet().stream()\n   303→                .filter(e -> tasks.contains(e.getKey()))\n   304→                .collect(Collectors.toMap(Map.Entry::getKey, Map.Entry::getValue));\n   305→    }\n   306→\n   307→    public void updateTaskOffsets(final TaskId taskId, final Map<TopicPartition, Long> changelogOffsets) {\n   308→        if (!changelogOffsets.isEmpty()) {\n   309→            taskOffsetSums.put(taskId, sumOfChangelogOffsets(taskId, changelogOffsets));\n   310→        }\n   311→    }\n   312→\n   313→    public void removeTaskOffsets(final TaskId taskId) {\n   314→        taskOffsetSums.remove(taskId);\n   315→    }\n   316→\n   317→    private long sumOfChangelogOffsets(final TaskId taskId, final Map<TopicPartition, Long> changelogOffsets) {\n   318→        long offsetSum = 0L;\n   319→        for (final Map.Entry<TopicPartition, Long> changelogEntry : changelogOffsets.entrySet()) {\n   320→            final long offset = changelogEntry.getValue();\n   321→\n   322→            if (offset != OffsetCheckpoint.OFFSET_UNKNOWN) {\n   323→                if (offset < 0) {\n   324→                    throw new StreamsException(\n   325→                            new IllegalStateException(\"Expected not to get a sentinel offset, but got: \" + changelogEntry),\n   326→                            taskId);\n   327→                }\n   328→                offsetSum += offset;\n   329→                if (offsetSum < 0) {\n   330→                    log.warn(\"Sum of changelog offsets for task {} overflowed, pinning to Long.MAX_VALUE\", taskId);\n   331→                    return Long.MAX_VALUE;\n   332→                }\n   333→            }\n   334→        }\n   335→\n   336→        return offsetSum;\n   337→    }\n   338→\n   339→    public UUID initializeProcessId() {\n   340→        if (!hasPersistentStores) {\n   341→            final UUID processId = UUID.randomUUID();\n   342→            log.info(\"Created new process id: {}\", processId);\n   343→            return processId;\n   344→        }\n   345→\n   346→        if (!lockStateDirectory()) {\n   347→            log.error(\"Unable to obtain lock as state directory is already locked by another process\");\n   348→            throw new StreamsException(String.format(\"Unable to initialize state, this can happen if multiple instances of \" +\n   349→                                           \"Kafka Streams are running in the same state directory \" +\n   350→                                           \"(current state directory is [%s]\", stateDir.getAbsolutePath()));\n   351→        }\n   352→\n   353→        final File processFile = new File(stateDir, PROCESS_FILE_NAME);\n   354→        final ObjectMapper mapper = new ObjectMapper();\n   355→\n   356→        try {\n   357→            if (processFile.exists()) {\n   358→                try {\n   359→                    final StateDirectoryProcessFile processFileData = mapper.readValue(processFile, StateDirectoryProcessFile.class);\n   360→                    log.info(\"Reading UUID from process file: {}\", processFileData.processId);\n   361→                    if (processFileData.processId != null) {\n   362→                        return processFileData.processId;\n   363→                    }\n   364→                } catch (final Exception e) {\n   365→                    log.warn(\"Failed to read json process file\", e);\n   366→                }\n   367→            }\n   368→\n   369→            final StateDirectoryProcessFile processFileData = new StateDirectoryProcessFile(UUID.randomUUID());\n   370→            log.info(\"No process id found on disk, got fresh process id {}\", processFileData.processId);\n   371→\n   372→            mapper.writeValue(processFile, processFileData);\n   373→            return processFileData.processId;\n   374→        } catch (final IOException e) {\n   375→            log.error(\"Unable to read/write process file due to unexpected exception\", e);\n   376→            throw new ProcessorStateException(e);\n   377→        }\n   378→    }\n   379→\n   380→    /**\n   381→     * Get or create the directory for the provided {@link TaskId}.\n   382→     * @return directory for the {@link TaskId}\n   383→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the task directory does not exist and could not be created\n   384→     */\n   385→    public File getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(final TaskId taskId) {\n   386→        final File taskParentDir = getTaskDirectoryParentName(taskId);\n   387→        final File taskDir = new File(taskParentDir, StateManagerUtil.toTaskDirString(taskId));\n   388→        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n   389→            if (!taskDir.exists()) {\n   390→                synchronized (taskDirCreationLock) {\n   391→                    // to avoid a race condition, we need to check again if the directory does not exist:\n   392→                    // otherwise, two threads might pass the outer `if` (and enter the `then` block),\n   393→                    // one blocks on `synchronized` while the other creates the directory,\n   394→                    // and the blocking one fails when trying to create it after it's unblocked\n   395→                    if (!taskParentDir.exists() && !taskParentDir.mkdir()) {\n   396→                        throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   397→                            String.format(\"Parent [%s] of task directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\",\n   398→                                taskParentDir.getPath(), taskDir.getPath()));\n   399→                    }\n   400→                    if (!taskDir.exists() && !taskDir.mkdir()) {\n   401→                        throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   402→                            String.format(\"task directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", taskDir.getPath()));\n   403→                    }\n   404→                }\n   405→            } else if (!taskDir.isDirectory()) {\n   406→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   407→                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", taskDir.getPath()));\n   408→            }\n   409→        }\n   410→        return taskDir;\n   411→    }\n   412→\n   413→    private File getTaskDirectoryParentName(final TaskId taskId) {\n   414→        final String namedTopology = taskId.topologyName();\n   415→        if (namedTopology != null) {\n   416→            if (!hasNamedTopologies) {\n   417→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Tried to lookup taskId with named topology, but StateDirectory thinks hasNamedTopologies = false\");\n   418→            }\n   419→            return new File(stateDir, getNamedTopologyDirName(namedTopology));\n   420→        } else {\n   421→            return stateDir;\n   422→        }\n   423→    }\n   424→\n   425→    private String getNamedTopologyDirName(final String topologyName) {\n   426→        return \"__\" + topologyName + \"__\";\n   427→    }\n   428→\n   429→    /**\n   430→     * @return The File handle for the checkpoint in the given task's directory\n   431→     */\n   432→    File checkpointFileFor(final TaskId taskId) {\n   433→        return new File(getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(taskId), StateManagerUtil.CHECKPOINT_FILE_NAME);\n   434→    }\n   435→\n   436→    /**\n   437→     * Decide if the directory of the task is empty or not\n   438→     */\n   439→    boolean directoryForTaskIsEmpty(final TaskId taskId) {\n   440→        final File taskDir = getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(taskId);\n   441→\n   442→        return taskDirIsEmpty(taskDir);\n   443→    }\n   444→\n   445→    private boolean taskDirIsEmpty(final File taskDir) {\n   446→        final File[] storeDirs = taskDir.listFiles(pathname ->\n   447→                !pathname.getName().equals(CHECKPOINT_FILE_NAME));\n   448→\n   449→        boolean taskDirEmpty = true;\n   450→\n   451→        // if the task is stateless, storeDirs would be null\n   452→        if (storeDirs != null && storeDirs.length > 0) {\n   453→            for (final File file : storeDirs) {\n   454→                // We removed the task directory locking but some upgrading applications may still have old lock files on disk,\n   455→                // we just lazily delete those in this method since it's the only thing that would be affected by these\n   456→                if (file.getName().endsWith(LOCK_FILE_NAME)) {\n   457→                    if (!file.delete()) {\n   458→                        // If we hit an error deleting this just ignore it and move on, we'll retry again at some point\n   459→                        log.warn(\"Error encountered deleting lock file in {}\", taskDir);\n   460→                    }\n   461→                } else {\n   462→                    // If it's not a lock file then the directory is not empty,\n   463→                    // but finish up the loop in case there's a lock file left to delete\n   464→                    log.trace(\"TaskDir {} was not empty, found {}\", taskDir, file);\n   465→                    taskDirEmpty = false;\n   466→                }\n   467→            }\n   468→        }\n   469→        return taskDirEmpty;\n   470→    }\n   471→\n   472→    /**\n   473→     * Get or create the directory for the global stores.\n   474→     * @return directory for the global stores\n   475→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the global store directory does not exists and could not be created\n   476→     */\n   477→    File globalStateDir() {\n   478→        final File dir = new File(stateDir, \"global\");\n   479→        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n   480→            if (!dir.exists() && !dir.mkdir()) {\n   481→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   482→                    String.format(\"global state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", dir.getPath()));\n   483→            } else if (dir.exists() && !dir.isDirectory()) {\n   484→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   485→                    String.format(\"global state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", dir.getPath()));\n   486→            }\n   487→        }\n   488→        return dir;\n   489→    }\n   490→\n   491→    private String logPrefix() {\n   492→        return String.format(\"stream-thread [%s]\", Thread.currentThread().getName());\n   493→    }\n   494→\n   495→    /**\n   496→     * Get the lock for the {@link TaskId}s directory if it is available\n   497→     * @param taskId task id\n   498→     * @return true if successful\n   499→     */\n   500→    synchronized boolean lock(final TaskId taskId) {\n   501→        if (!hasPersistentStores) {\n   502→            return true;\n   503→        }\n   504→\n   505→        final Thread lockOwner = lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n   506→        if (lockOwner != null) {\n   507→            if (lockOwner.equals(Thread.currentThread())) {\n   508→                log.trace(\"{} Found cached state dir lock for task {}\", logPrefix(), taskId);\n   509→                // we already own the lock\n   510→                return true;\n   511→            } else {\n   512→                // another thread owns the lock\n   513→                return false;\n   514→            }\n   515→        } else if (!stateDir.exists()) {\n   516→            log.error(\"Tried to lock task directory for {} but the state directory does not exist\", taskId);\n   517→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"The state directory has been deleted\");\n   518→        } else {\n   519→            lockedTasksToOwner.put(taskId, Thread.currentThread());\n   520→            return true;\n   521→        }\n   522→    }\n   523→\n   524→    /**\n   525→     * Unlock the state directory for the given {@link TaskId}.\n   526→     */\n   527→    synchronized void unlock(final TaskId taskId) {\n   528→        final Thread lockOwner = lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n   529→        if (lockOwner != null && lockOwner.equals(Thread.currentThread())) {\n   530→            lockedTasksToOwner.remove(taskId);\n   531→            log.debug(\"{} Released state dir lock for task {}\", logPrefix(), taskId);\n   532→        }\n   533→    }\n   534→\n   535→    /**\n   536→     * Expose for tests.\n   537→     */\n   538→    Thread lockOwner(final TaskId taskId) {\n   539→        return lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n   540→    }\n   541→\n   542→    @Override\n   543→    public void close() {\n   544→        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n   545→            unlockStartupStores();\n   546→            taskOffsetSums.clear();\n   547→            try {\n   548→                stateDirLock.release();\n   549→                stateDirLockChannel.close();\n   550→\n   551→                stateDirLock = null;\n   552→                stateDirLockChannel = null;\n   553→            } catch (final IOException e) {\n   554→                log.error(\"Unexpected exception while unlocking the state dir\", e);\n   555→                throw new StreamsException(String.format(\"Failed to release the lock on the state directory [%s]\", stateDir.getAbsolutePath()), e);\n   556→            }\n   557→\n   558→            // all threads should be stopped and cleaned up by now, so none should remain holding a lock\n   559→            if (!lockedTasksToOwner.isEmpty()) {\n   560→                log.error(\"Some task directories still locked while closing state, this indicates unclean shutdown: {}\", lockedTasksToOwner);\n   561→            }\n   562→        }\n   563→    }\n   564→\n   565→    public synchronized void clean() {\n   566→        try {\n   567→            cleanStateAndTaskDirectoriesCalledByUser();\n   568→        } catch (final Exception e) {\n   569→            throw new StreamsException(e);\n   570→        }\n   602→    /**\n   603→     * Remove the directories for any {@link TaskId}s that are no-longer\n   604→     * owned by this {@link StreamThread} and aren't locked by either\n   605→     * another }\n   643→            }\n   644→        }\n   645→        // Ok to ignore returned exception as it should be swallowed\n   646→        maybeCleanEmptyNamedTopologyDirs(true);\n   647→    }\n   648→\n           processed = task.process(now);\n   155→                if (processed) {\n   156→                    log.trace(\"Successfully processed task {}\", task.id());\n   157→              throw new StreamsException(e, task.id());\n   183→            } finally {\n   184→                task.recordProcessBatchTime(time.milliseconds() - now);\n   185→            }\n   186→ taskManager.setUncaughtException(new StreamsException(e, currentTask.id()), currentTask.id());\n   204→                }\n   205→            }\n   206→            taskManager.unassignTask(currentTask,      private volatile KafkaFutureImpl<Uuid> clientInstanceIdFuture = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n   104→\n   105→        public StateUpdaterThread(final String name,\n   106→ 113→\n   114→        public Collection<Task> updatingTasks() {\n   115→            return updatingTasks.values();\n   116→        }\n   117→\n   118→        public Collection<StandbyTask>           .collect(Collectors.toList());\n   123→        }\n   124→\n   125→        private boolean onlyStandbyTasksUpdating() {\n   126→            return !updatingTasks.isEmpty() &&       }\n   132→\n   133→        public long numUpdatingStandbyTasks() {\n   134→            return updatingTasks.values().stream()\n   135→                .filter(t -> !t.isActive())\n   136→                .count();\n   137→        }\n   138→\n   139→        public long numRestoringActiveTasks() {\n   140→            return updatingTasks.values().stream()\n   141→                .filter(Task::isActive)\n   142→                .count();\n   143→        }\n   144→\n   145→        public long numPausedStandbyTasks() {\n   146→            return pausedTasks.values().stream()\n {\n   159→            log.info(\"State updater thread started\");\n   160→            try {\n   161→                initTimeWindowIfNeeded(time.milliseconds());\n   162→                while (isRunning.get()) {\n   163→                    runOnce();\n   164→                }\n   165→            } catch (final RuntimeException anyOtherException) {\n   166→                handleRuntimeException(anyOtherException);\n   167→            } finally {\n   168→                clearInputQueue();\n   169→                clearUpdatingAndPausedTasks();\n   170→                updaterMetrics.clear();\n   171→                log.info(\"State updater thread stopped\");\n   172→            }\n   173→        }\n   174→\n   175→        private void clearInputQueue() {\n   176→            tasksAndActionsLock.lock();\n   177→            try {\n   178→                tasksAndActions.clear();\n   179→            } finally {\n   180→                tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n   181→            }\n   182→        }\n   183→\n   184→        // In each iteration:\n   185→        //   1) check if updating tasks need to be paused\n   186→        //   2) check if paused tasks need to be resumed\n   187→        //   3) restore those updating tasks\n   188→        //   4) checkpoint those updating task states\n   189→        //   5) idle waiting if there is no more tasks to be restored\n   190→        //\n   191→        //   Note that, 1-3) are measured as restoring time, while 4) and 5) measured separately\n   192→        //   as checkpointing time and idle time\n   193→        private void runOnce() {\n   194→            final long totalStartTimeMs = time.milliseconds();\n   195→            performActionsOnTasks();\n   196→\n   197→            resumeTasks();\n   198→ Thread A holds StateDirectory's intrinsic lock (e.g., via `cleanRemovedTasks()`) and calls:\n   - `cleanRemovedTasksCalledByCleanerThread()` (line 617) → \n   - `lock(id)` (line 623) which tries to enter synchronized block but **already owns it** (reentrant is okay)\n   - But then within the synchronized block, it may call `removeStartupState(id)` (line 630)\n   - `removeStartupState()` is also synchronized, causing potential issues\n\n2. Thread B (task processor) calls `getOrCreateDirectoryForTask()` which needs:\n   - `taskDirCreationLock` (inner synchronization, line 390)\n   - If thread B then calls a state manager method that tries to acquire task lock via `StateDirectory.lock()`, it needs StateDirectory's synchronized lock\n\n3. Meanwhile, a cleaner thread may hold `StateDirectory`'s synchronized lock while trying to manipulate task state\n\n---\n\n## DEADLOCK SCENARIO 2: DefaultStateUpdater Multiple Lock Acquisition Order\n\n**Location**: `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`\n\n**Critical Method**: `executeWithQueuesLocked()` (lines 1092-1103)\n\n```java\nprivate <T> Set<T> executeWithQueuesLocked(final Supplier<Set<T>> action) {\n    tasksAndActionsLock.lock();           // Lock 1\n    restoredActiveTasksLock.lock();       // Lock 2\n    exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock.lock();  // Lock 3\n    try {\n        return action.get();\n    } finally {\n        exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock.unlock();\n        restoredActiveTasksLock.unlock();\n        tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Circular Dependency Potential**:\n1. This method acquires **three locks in a fixed order**: `tasksAndActionsLock` → `restoredActiveTasksLock` → `exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock`\n2. Other methods acquire the same locks in **different orders**:\n   - `performActionsOnTasks()` (line 217): Only `tasksAndActionsLock`\n   - `addToExceptionsAndFailedTasks*()` methods: Only `exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock`\n   - `addToRestoredTasks()` (line 708): Only `restoredActiveTasksLock`\n\n3. If a callback or notification happens while one lock is held and tries to acquire another in reverse order, deadlock occurs.\n\n**Example Scenario**:\n- Thread A: Holds `tasksAndActionsLock`, tries to get state task info that needs `restoredActiveTasksLock`\n- Thread B: Holds `restoredActiveTasksLock`, tries to process actions that need `tasksAndActionsLock`\n\n---\n\n## DEADLOCK SCENARIO 3: StateDirectory.cleanRemovedTasks() and Task Lock Interaction\n\n**Location**: `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/StateDirectory.java` (lines 609-642)\n\n**Lock Sequence Problem**:\n```java\npublic synchronized void cleanRemovedTasks(final long cleanupDelayMs) {\n    // Holds StateDirectory's synchronized lock\n    try {\n        cleanRemovedTasksCalledByCleanerThread(cleanupDelayMs);\n    } catch (final Exception cannotHappen) {\n        ...\n    }\n}\n\nprivate void cleanRemovedTasksCalledByCleanerThread(final long cleanupDelayMs) {\n    for (final TaskDirectory taskDir : listAllTaskDirectories()) {\n        ...\n        if (lock(id)) {                    // Tries to acquire task lock\n            ...\n            removeStartupState(id);        // Synchronized method - problem!\n            ...\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Issue**: \n- `cleanRemovedTasks()` holds StateDirectory's instance lock\n- Calls `lock(id)` which is also synchronized on the same StateDirectory instance\n- Since `lock()` is synchronized but called from within another synchronized method on the same object, this could deadlock if the same lock is needed by another thread trying to call these in reverse order\n\n---\n\n## DEADLOCK SCENARIO 4: KafkaStreams State Management and Task Cleanup Race\n\n**Location**: `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/KafkaStreams.java`\n\n**Lock Interaction**:\n1. `KafkaStreams.close()` is synchronized (line 1609) and holds `stateLock`\n2. During close, it may call `stateDirectory.clean()` which is also synchronized (StateDirectory instance lock)\n3. Concurrently, a scheduled `stateDirCleaner` thread calls `stateDirectory.cleanRemovedTasks()` \n4. If both threads try to acquire StateDirectory's synchronized lock while other threads hold task-level locks, circular wait forms\n\n**Problematic Code Section** (lines 1405-1410):\n```java\nstateDirCleaner.scheduleAtFixedRate(() -> {\n    if (state == State.RUNNING) {\n        stateDirectory.cleanRemovedTasks(cleanupDelay);  // Synchronized method\n    }\n}, cleanupDelay, cleanupDelay, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n```\n\n---\n\n## DEADLOCK SCENARIO 5: DefaultStateUpdater and StateDirectory Lock Ordering\n\n**Critical Interaction**: When `DefaultStateUpdater` processes task state transitions while `StateDirectory` cleaning threads run:\n\n1. `DefaultStateUpdater.performActionsOnTasks()` (line 217) locks `tasksAndActionsLock`\n2. During task transitions, may need to call `stateDirectory.lock()` which is synchronized\n3. Meanwhile, `StateDirectory.cleanRemovedTasks()` (synchronized) tries to access task state\n4. If task state callback needs to acquire any of the three DefaultStateUpdater locks, **deadlock**\n\n---\n\n## Key Files with Deadlock Vulnerabilities\n\n1. **`/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/StateDirectory.java`**\n   - Multiple synchronized methods: `lock()`, `unlock()`, `removeStartupState()`, `clean()`, `cleanRemovedTasks()`\n   - Inner synchronization on `taskDirCreationLock`\n   - Nested synchronized calls from `cleanRemovedTasks()` → `lock()` → `removeStartupState()` on same instance\n\n2. **`/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`**\n   - This is possible if we do not have enough executors or because those tasks\n    52→ *    are not processable (e.g. because no records fetched) yet.\n    53→ */\n    54→public final class DefaultTaskManager implements TaskManager {\n    55→\n    56→    private final Time time;\n    57→    private final Logger log;\n    58→    private final TasksRegistry tasks;\n    59→\n    60→    private final Lock tasksLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    61→    private final Condition tasksCondition = tasksLock.newCondition();\n    62→    private final List<TaskId> lockedTasks = new ArrayList<>();\n    63→    private final Map<TaskId, StreamsException> uncaughtExceptions = new HashMap<>();\n    64→    private final Map<TaskId, TaskExecutor> assignedTasks = new HashMap<>();\n    65→    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    66→\n    67→    private final List<TaskExecutor> taskExecutors;\n    68→\n    69→    public static class DefaultTaskExecutorCreator implements TaskExecutorCreator {\n    70→        @Override\n    71→        public TaskExecutor create(final TaskManager taskManager, final String name, final Time time, final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n    72→            return new DefaultTaskExecutor(taskManager, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata);\n    73→        }\n    74→    }\n    75→\n    76→    public DefaultTaskManager(final Time time,\n    77→                              final String clientId,\n    78→                              final TasksRegistry tasks,\n    79→                              final TaskExecutorCreator executorCreator,\n    80→                              final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata,\n    81→                              final int numExecutors\n    82→                              ) {\n    83→        final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", clientId);\n    84→        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n    85→        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskManager.class);\n    86→        this.time = time;\n    87→        this.tasks = tasks;\n    88→        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n    89→\n    90→        this.taskExecutors = new ArrayList<>(numExecutors);\n    91→        for (int i = 1; i <= numExecutors; i++) {\n    92→            final String name = clientId + \"-TaskExecutor-\" + i;\n    93→            this.taskExecutors.add(executorCreator.create(this, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata));\n    94→        }\n    95→    }\n    96→\n    97→    @Override\n    98→    public StreamTask assignNextTask(final TaskExecutor executor) {\n    99→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   100→            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n   101→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for getting next task to assign is unrecognized\");\n   102→            }\n   103→\n   104→            // the most naive scheduling algorithm for now: give the next unlocked, unassigned, and  processable task\n   105→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   106→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   107→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   108→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   109→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   110→                ) {\n   111→\n   112→                    assignedTasks.put(task.id(), executor);\n   113→\n   114→                    log.debug(\"Assigned task {} to executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   115→\n   116→                    return task;\n   117→                }\n   118→            }\n   119→\n   120→            log.debug(\"Found no assignable task for executor {}\", executor.name());\n   121→\n   122→            return null;\n   123→        });\n   124→    }\n   125→\n   126→    @Override\n   127→    public void awaitProcessableTasks(final Supplier<Boolean> isShuttingDown) throws InterruptedException {\n   128→        final boolean interrupted = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   129→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   130→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   131→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   132→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   133→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   134→                ) {\n   135→                    log.debug(\"Await unblocked: returning early from await since a processable task {} was found\", task.id());\n   136→                    return false;\n   137→                }\n   138→            }\n   139→            try {\n   140→                // We re-check the shutdownRequest atomic boolean to avoid a race condition. If this thread was\n   141→                // previously interrupted while awaiting tasksCondition, it is possible to miss the signalAll that\n   142→                // is called during shutdown. If this happens, we end up blocking in the await forever.\n   143→                if (!isShuttingDown.get()) {\n   144→                    log.debug(\"Await blocking\");\n   145→                    tasksCondition.await();\n   146→                } else {\n   147→                    log.debug(\"Not awaiting since shutdown was requested\");\n   148→                }\n   149→            } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n   150→                // we interrupt the thread for shut down and pause.\n   151→                // we can ignore this exception.\n   152→                log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Interrupted while waiting for processable tasks\");\n   153→                return true;\n   154→            }\n   155→            log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Woken up to check for processable tasks\");\n   156→            return false;\n   157→        });\n   158→\n   159→        if (interrupted) {\n   160→            throw new InterruptedException();\n   161→        }\n   162→    }\n   163→\n   164→    public void signalTaskExecutors() {\n   165→        log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   166→        executeWithTasksLocked(tasksCondition::signalAll);\n   167→    }\n   168→\n   169→    @Override\n   170→    public void unassignTask(final StreamTask task, final TaskExecutor executor) {\n   171→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   172→            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n   173→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for unassign task is unrecognized\");\n   174→            }\n   175→\n   176→            final TaskExecutor lockedExecutor = assignedTasks.get(task.id());\n   177→            if (lockedExecutor == null || lockedExecutor != executor) {\n   178→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Task \" + task.id() + \" is not locked by the executor\");\n   179→            }\n   180→\n   181→            assignedTasks.remove(task.id());\n   182→\n   183→            log.debug(\"Unassigned {} from executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   184→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   185→        });\n   186→    }\n   187→\n   188→    @Override\n   189→    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   190→        final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> result = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n   191→\n   192→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   193→            result.complete(null);\n   194→            return result;\n   195→        }\n   196→\n   197→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   198→            lockedTasks.addAll(taskIds);\n   199→\n   200→            final Set<TaskId> remainingTaskIds = new ConcurrentSkipListSet<>(taskIds);\n   201→\n   202→            for (final TaskId taskId : taskIds) {\n   203→                final Task task = tasks.initializedTask(taskId);\n   204→\n   205→                if (task == null) {\n   206→                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Trying to lock task \" + taskId + \" but it's not owned\");\n   207→                }\n   208→\n   209→                if (!task.isActive()) {\n   210→                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The locking task \" + taskId + \" is not an active task\");\n   211→                }\n   212→\n   213→                if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   214→                    final TaskExecutor executor = assignedTasks.get(taskId);\n   215→                    log.debug(\"Requesting release of task {} from {}\", taskId, executor.name());\n   216→                    final KafkaFuture<StreamTask> future = executor.unassign();\n   217→                    future.whenComplete((streamTask, throwable) -> {\n   218→                        if (throwable != null) {\n   219→                            result.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n   220→                        } else {\n   221→                            assert !assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId);\n   222→                            // It can happen that the executor handed back the task before we asked it to\n   223→                            // in which case `streamTask` will be null here.\n   224→                            assert streamTask == null || streamTask.id() == taskId;\n   225→                            remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   226→                            if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   227→                                result.complete(null);\n   228→                            }\n   229→                        }\n   230→                    });\n   231→                } else {\n   232→                    remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   233→                    if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   234→                        result.complete(null);\n   235→                    }\n   236→                }\n   237→            }\n   238→\n   239→            return result;\n   240→        });\n   241→    }\n   242→\n   243→    @Override\n   244→    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockAllTasks() {\n   245→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() ->\n   246→            lockTasks(tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(Task::id).collect(Collectors.toSet()))\n   247→        );\n   248→    }\n   249→\n   250→    @Override\n   251→    public void unlockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   252→\n   253→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   254→            return;\n   255→        }\n   256→\n   257→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   258→            lockedTasks.removeAll(taskIds);\n   259→            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   260→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   261→        });\n   262→    }\n   263→\n   264→    @Override\n   265→    public void unlockAllTasks() {\n   266→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> 275→            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   276→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   277→        });\n   278→\n   279→        log.info(\"Added tasks {} to the task manager to process\", tasksToAdd);\n   280→    }\n   281→\n   282→    @Override\n   283→    public void remove(final TaskId taskId) {\n   284→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   285→            if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   286→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is still assigned to executors\");\n   287→            }\n   288→\n   289→            if (!lockedTasks.contains(taskId)) {\n   290→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not locked yet by the task manager\");\n   291→            }\n   292→\n   293→            if (!tasks.containsInitialized(taskId)) {\n   294→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not owned by the task manager\");\n   295→            }\n   296→\n   297→            tasks.removeTask(tasks.initializedTask(taskId));\n   298→        });\n   299→\n   300→        log.info(\"Removed task {} from the task manager\", taskId);\n   301→    }\n   302→\n   303→    @Override\n   304→    public Set<ReadOnlyTask> getTasks() {\n   305→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(ReadOnlyTask::new).collect(Collectors.toSet()));\n   306→    }\n   307→\n   308→    @Override\n   309→    public void setUncaughtException(final StreamsException exception, final TaskId taskId) {\n   310→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   311→\n   312→            if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   313→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"An uncaught exception can only be set as long as the task is still assigned\");\n   314→            }\n   315→\n   316→            if (uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   317→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The uncaught exception must be cleared before restarting processing\");\n   318→            }\n   319→\n   320→            uncaughtExceptions.put(taskId, exception);\n   321→        });\n   322→\n   323→        log.info(\"Set an uncaught exception of type {} for task {}, with error message: {}\",\n   324→            exception.getClass().getName(),\n   325→            taskId,\n   326→            exception.getMessage());\n   327→    }\n   328→\n   329→    public Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> drainUncaughtExceptions() {\n   330→        final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> returnValue = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   331→            final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> result = new HashMap<>(uncaughtExceptions);\n   332→            uncaughtExceptions.clear();\n   333→            return result;\n   334→        });\n   335→\n   336→        if (!returnValue.isEmpty()) {\n   337→            log.debug(\"Drained {} uncaught exceptions\", returnValue.size());\n   338→        }\n   339→\n   340→        return returnValue;\n   341→    }\n   342→\n   343→    public boolean hasUncaughtException(final TaskId taskId) {\n   344→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId));\n   345→    }\n   346→\n   347→    private void executeWithTasksLocked(final Runnable action) {\n   348→        tasksLock.lock();\n   349→        try {\n   350→            action.run();\n   351→        } finally {\n   352→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   353→        }\n   354→    }\n   355→\n   356→    private <T> T returnWithTasksLocked(final Supplier<T> action) {\n   357→        tasksLock.lock();\n   358→        try {\n   359→            return action.get();\n   360→        } finally {\n   361→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   362→        }\n   363→    }\n   364→\n   365→    private boolean canProgress(final StreamTask task, final long nowMs) {\n   366→        return\n   367→            taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(task, nowMs) && task.isProcessable(nowMs) ||\n   368→                taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(task) && (task.canPunctuateStreamTime() || task.canPunctuateSystemTime());\n   369→    }\n   370→\n   371→    public void startTaskExecutors() {\n   372→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n   373→            t.start();\n   374→        }\n   375→    }\n   376→\n   377→    public void shutdown(final Duration duration) {\n   378→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n   379→            t.requestShutdown();\n   380→        }\n   381→        signalTaskExecutors();\n   382→        for (final TaskExecutor t: taskExecutors) {\n   383→            t.awaitShutdown(duration);\n   384→        }\n   385→    }\n   386→}\n
