   private Thread shutdownHelper(\n        final boolean error,\n        final long timeoutMs,\n        final org.apache.kafka.streams.CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation operation\n    ) {\n        stateDirCleaner.shutdownNow();\n        if (rocksDBMetricsRecordingService != null) {\n            rocksDBMetricsRecordingService.shutdownNow();\n        }\n\n        // wait for all threads to join in a separate thread;\n        // save the current thread so that if it is a stream thread\n        // we don't attempt to join it and cause a deadlock\n        return new Thread(() -> {\n            // notify all the threads to stop; avoid deadlocks by stopping any\n            // further state reports from the thread since we're shutting down\n            int numStreamThreads = processStreamThread(\n                streamThread -> streamThread.shutdown(operation)\n            );\n\n            log.info(\"Shutting down {} stream threads\", numStreamThreads);\n\n            topologyMetadata.wakeupThreads();\n\n            numStreamThreads = processStreamThread(thread -> {\n                try {\n                    if (!thread.isRunning()) {\n                        log.debug(\"Shutdown {} complete\", thread.getName());\n\n                        thread.join();\n                    }\n                } catch (final InterruptedException ex) {\n                    log.warn(\"Shutdown {} interrupted\", thread.getName());\n\n                    Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                }\n            });\n\n            log.info(\"Shutdown {} stream threads complete\", numStreamThreads);\n\n            if (globalStreamThread != null) {\n                log.info(\"Shutting down the global stream threads\");\n\n                globalStreamThread.shutdown();\n            }\n\n            if (globalStreamThread != null && !globalStreamThread.stillRunning()) {\n                try {\n                    globalStreamThread.join();\n                } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n                    log.warn(\"Shutdown the global stream thread interrupted\");\n\n                    Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                }\n                globalStreamThread = null;\n\n                log.info(\"Shutdown global stream threads complete\");\n            }\n\n            stateDirectory.close();\n            // passing in `timeoutMs` here is a quick fix to avoid blocking forever\n            // cf https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/KAFKA-7996\n            if (timeoutMs >= 0) {\n                adminClient.close(Duration.ofMillis(timeoutMs));\n            } else {\n                adminClient.close();\n            }\n\n            streamsMetrics.removeAllClientLevelSensorsAndMetrics();\n            metrics.close();\n            if (!error) {\n                setState(State.NOT_RUNNING);\n            } else {\n                setState(State.ERROR);\n            }\n        }, clientId + \"-CloseThread\");\n    }\n\n    // visible for testing\n    boolean close(final Optional<Long> timeout, final org.apache.kafka.streams.CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation operation) {\n        final long timeoutMs;\n        if (timeout.isPresent()) {\n            timeoutMs = timeout.get();\n            log.debug(\"Stopping Streams client with timeout = {}ms.\", timeoutMs);\n        } else {\n            timeoutMs = Long.MAX_VALUE;\n        }\n\n        if (!setState(State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN)) {\n            // Copy the state so that we can atomically check if we are shut down and act on it (log it)\n            final State immutableStateCopy = state;\n            if (immutableStateCopy.isShuttingDown()) {\n                log.info(\"Skipping shutdown since Streams client is already in {}, waiting for a terminal state\", immutableStateCopy);\n                if (!waitOnStates(timeoutMs, State.ERROR, State.NOT_RUNNING)) {\n                    log.warn(\"Streams client did transition to a terminal state (ERROR or NOT_RUNNING) within the {}ms timeout\", timeoutMs);\n                    return false;\n                }\n                log.info(\"Streams client stopped completely and transitioned to the terminal {} state\", state);\n                return true;\n            }\n\n            if (state.hasCompletedShutdown()) {\n                log.info(\"Skipping shutdown since Streams client is already in the terminal {} state\", state);\n                return true;\n            }\n\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"If transitioning to PENDING_SHUTDOWN fails, the state should be either in \"\n                + \"PENDING_SHUTDOWN, PENDING_ERROR, ERROR, or NOT_RUNNING\");\n        }\n\n        final Thread shutdownThread = shutdownHelper(false, timeoutMs, operation);\n\n We will trim it from the\n                                // list once it reaches DEAD, and if for some reason it's hanging indefinitely in the\n                                // shutdown then we should just consider this thread.id to be burned\n                            } else {\n                                log.info(\"Successfully removed {} in {}ms\", streamThread.getName(), time.milliseconds() - startMs);\n                                threads.remove(streamThread);\n                                queryableStoreProvider.removeStoreProviderForThread(streamThread.getName());\n                            }\n                        } else {\n                            log.info(\"{} is the last remaining thread and must remove itself, therefore we cannot wait \"\n                                + \"for it to complete shutdown as this will result in deadlock.\", streamThread.getName());\n                        }\n\n                        final long cacheSizePerThread = cacheSizePerThread(numLiveStreamThreads());\n                        log.info(\"Resizing thread cache due to thread removal, new cache size per thread is {}\", cacheSizePerThread);\n                        resizeThreadCache(cacheSizePerThread);\n                        final long remainingTimeMs = timeoutMs - (time.milliseconds() - startMs);\n                        if (remainingTimeMs <= 0) {\n                            throw new TimeoutException(\"Thread \" + streamThread.getName() + \" did not stop in the allotted time\");\n                        }\n                        return Optional.of(streamThread.getName());\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            log.warn(\"There are no threads eligible for removal\");","numLines":130,"startLine":1100,"totalLines":2154}}}

private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(StateDirectory.class);\n    93→    static final String LOCK_FILE_NAME = \".lock\";\n    94→\n    95→    /* The process file is used to persist \"kafka-streams-process-metadata\";\n    99→\n   100→    @JsonIgnoreProperties(ignoreUnknown = true)\n   101→    static class StateDirectoryProcessFile {\n   102→        @JsonProperty\n   103→    private final StreamsConfig config;\n   128→    private final Set<TaskId> tasksInLocalState = new ConcurrentSkipListSet<>();\n   129→    private final Map<TaskId, Long> taskOffsetSums = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\n   130→\n   131→    /**\n   132→     * Ensures that the state base directory as well as the application's sub-directory are created.\n   133→     *\n   134→     * @param config              streams application configuration to read the root state directory path\n   135→     * @param time                system timer used to execute periodic cleanup procedure\n   136→     * @param hasPersistentStores only when the application's topology does have stores persisted on local file\n   137→     *                            system, we would go ahead and auto-create the corresponding application / task / store\n   138→     *                            directories whenever necessary; otherwise no directories would be created.\n   139→     * @param hasNamedTopologies  whether this application is composed of independent named topologies\n   140→     *\n   141→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the base state directory or application state directory does not exist\n   142→     *                                 and could not be created when hasPersistentStores is enabled.\n   143→     */\n   144→    public StateDirectory(final StreamsConfig config, final Time time, final boolean hasPersistentStores, final boolean hasNamedTopologies) {\n   145→        this.time = time;\n   146→        this.hasPersistentStores = hasPersistentStores;\n   147→        this.hasNamedTopologies = hasNamedTopologies;\n   148→        this.appId = config.getString(StreamsConfig.APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG);\n   149→        this.config = config;\n   150→        final String stateDirName = config.getString(StreamsConfig.STATE_DIR_CONFIG);\n   151→        final File baseDir = new File(stateDirName);\n   152→        stateDir = new File(baseDir, appId);\n   153→\n   154→        if (this.hasPersistentStores) {\n   155→            if (!baseDir.exists() && !baseDir.mkdirs()) {\n   156→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   157→                    String.format(\"base state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", stateDirName));\n   158→            }\n   159→            if (!stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.mkdir()) {\n   160→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   161→                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", stateDir.getPath()));\n   162→            } else if (stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.isDirectory()) {\n   163→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   164→                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", stateDir.getPath()));\n   165→            }\n   166→\n   167→            if (stateDirName.startsWith(System.getProperty(\"java.io.tmpdir\"))) {\n   168→                log.warn(\"Using an OS temp directory in the state.dir property can cause failures with writing\" +\n   169→                    \" the checkpoint file due to the fact that this directory can be cleared by the OS.\" +\n   170→                    \" Resolved state.dir: [\" + stateDirName + \"]\");\n   171→\n   172→            }\n   173→            // change the dir permission to \"rwxr-x---\" to avoid world readable\n   174→            configurePermissions(baseDir);\n   175→            configurePermissions(stateDir);\n   176→        }\n   177→    }\n   178→\n   179→    private void configurePermissions(final File file) {\n   180→        final Path path = file.toPath();\n   181→        if (path.getFileSystem().supportedFileAttributeViews().contains(\"posix\")) {\n   182→            final Set<PosixFilePermission> perms = EnumSet.of(\n   183→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_READ,\n   184→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_WRITE,\n   185→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_EXECUTE,\n   186→                    PosixFilePermission.GROUP_READ,\n   187→                    PosixFilePermission.GROUP_EXECUTE\n   188→            );\n   189→            if (config.getBoolean(StreamsConfig.ALLOW_OS_GROUP_WRITE_ACCESS_CONFIG)) {\n   190→                perms.add(PosixFilePermission.GROUP_WRITE);\n   191→            }\n   192→            try {\n   193→                Files.setPosixFilePermissions(path, perms);\n   194→            } catch (final IOException e) {\n   195→                log.error(\"Error changing permissions for the directory {} \", path, e);\n   196→            }\n   197→        } else {\n   198→            boolean set = file.setReadable(true, true);\n   199→            set &= file.setWritable(true, true);\n   200→            set &= file.setExecutable(true, true);\n   201→            if (!set) {\n   202→                log.error(\"Failed to change permissions for the directory {}\", file);\n   203→            }\n   204→        }\n   205→    }\n   206→\n   207→    /**\n   208→     * @return true if the state directory was successfully locked\n   209→     */\n   210→    private boolean lockStateDirectory() {\n   211→        final File lockFile = new File(stateDir, LOCK_FILE_NAME);\n   212→        try {\n   213→            stateDirLockChannel = FileChannel.open(lockFile.toPath(), StandardOpenOption.CREATE, StandardOpenOption.WRITE);\n   214→            stateDirLock = tryLock(stateDirLockChannel);\n   215→        } catch (final IOException e) {\n   216→            log.error(\"Unable to lock the state directory due to unexpected exception\", e);\n   217→            throw new ProcessorStateException(String.format(\"Failed to lock the state directory [%s] during startup\",\n   218→                stateDir.getAbsolutePath()), e);\n   219→        }\n   220→        return stateDirLock != null;\n   221→    }\n   222→\n   223→    /**\n   224→     * @throws LockException If another process already locks any of StateDirectory\n   225→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if any of task directory does not exist and could not be created\n   226→    */\n   227→    public void initializeStartupStores(final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata,\n   228→                                        final LogContext logContext,\n   229→                                        final StreamsMetricsImpl metricsImpl) {\n   230→        final List<TaskDirectory> nonEmptyTaskDirectories = listNonEmptyTaskDirectories();\n   231→        if (hasPersistentStores && !nonEmptyTaskDirectories.isEmpty()) {\n   232→            final boolean eosEnabled = StreamsConfigUtils.eosEnabled(config);\n   233→\n   234→            // Initialize thread-specific resources needed to open stores in the state directory\n   235→            final String threadLogPrefix = String.format(\"[%s]\", Thread.currentThread().getName());\n   236→            final ThreadCache dummyCache = new ThreadCache(new LogContext(threadLogPrefix), 0L, metricsImpl);\n   237→\n   238→            // discover all non-empty task directories in StateDirectory\n   239→            for (final TaskDirectory taskDirectory : nonEmptyTaskDirectories) {\n   240→                final String dirName = taskDirectory.file().getName();\n   241→                final TaskId task = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDirectory.namedTopology());\n   242→                final ProcessorTopology subTopology = topologyMetadata.buildSubtopology(task);\n   243→\n   244→                // we still check if the task's sub-topology is stateful, even though we know its directory contains state,\n   245→                // because it's possible that the topology has changed since that data was written, and is now stateless\n   246→                // this therefore prevents us from creating unnecessary stores just because of some left-over state\n   247→                if (subTopology.hasStateWithChangelogs()) {\n   248→                    final Set<TopicPartition> inputPartitions = topologyMetadata.nodeToSourceTopics(task).values().stream()\n   249→                            .flatMap(Collection::stream)\n   250→                            .map(t -> new TopicPartition(t, task.partition()))\n   251→                            .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n   252→                    // Open a temporary state manager that will open the stores inside the subtopology\n   253→                    final ProcessorStateManager temporaryStateManager = ProcessorStateManager.createStartupTaskStateManager(\n   254→                        task,\n   255→                        eosEnabled,\n   256→                        logContext,\n   257→                        this,\n   258→                        subTopology.storeToChangelogTopic(),\n   259→                        inputPartitions\n   260→                    );\n   261→\n   262→                    final StartupContext initContext = new StartupContext(task, config, temporaryStateManager, metricsImpl, dummyCache);\n   263→                    try {\n   264→                        // We only handle TaskCorruptedException at this point. Any other exception is considered fatal.\n   265→                        StateManagerUtil.registerStateStores(log, threadLogPrefix, subTopology, temporaryStateManager, this, initContext);\n   266→                        temporaryStateManager.checkpoint();\n   267→                    } catch (final TaskCorruptedException tce) {\n   268→                        // At this point, we only log a warning and continue with the startup store initialization.\n   269→                        // The task-corrupted exception will be handled in the first Task assignment phase.\n   270→                        log.warn(\"Failed to register startup state stores for task {}: {}\", task, tce.getMessage());\n   271→                    } finally {\n   272→                        // Make sure the state manager writes the local checkpoint file before closing the stores\n   273→                        // This will be replaced in the future when removing the checkpoint file dependency.\n   274→                        temporaryStateManager.close();\n   275→                    }\n   276→                    tasksInLocalState.add(task);\n   277→                }\n   278→            }\n   279→        }\n   280→    }\n   281→\n   282→    public boolean hasStartupTasks() {\n   283→        return !tasksInLocalState.isEmpty();\n   284→    }\n   285→\n   286→    public synchronized boolean removeStartupState(final TaskId taskId) {\n   287→        final boolean removed = tasksInLocalState.remove(taskId);\n   288→        if (removed) {\n   289→            lockedTasksToOwner.put(taskId, Thread.currentThread());\n   290→        }\n   291→        return removed;\n   292→    }\n   293→\n   294→\n   295→    private void unlockStartupStores() {\n   296→        for (final TaskId task : tasksInLocalState) {\n   297→            unlock(task);\n   298→        }\n   299→    }\n   300→\n   301→    public Map<TaskId, Long> taskOffsetSums(final Set<TaskId> tasks) {\n   302→        return taskOffsetSums.entrySet().stream()\n   303→                .filter(e -> tasks.contains(e.getKey()))\n   304→                .collect(Collectors.toMap(Map.Entry::getKey, Map.Entry::getValue));\n   305→    }\n   306→\n   307→    public void updateTaskOffsets(final TaskId taskId, final Map<TopicPartition, Long> changelogOffsets) {\n   308→        if (!changelogOffsets.isEmpty()) {\n   309→            taskOffsetSums.put(taskId, sumOfChangelogOffsets(taskId, changelogOffsets));\n   310→        }\n   311→    }\n   312→\n   313→    public void removeTaskOffsets(final TaskId taskId) {\n   314→        taskOffsetSums.remove(taskId);\n   315→    }\n   316→\n   317→    private long sumOfChangelogOffsets(final TaskId taskId, final Map<TopicPartition, Long> changelogOffsets) {\n   318→        long offsetSum = 0L;\n   319→        for (final Map.Entry<TopicPartition, Long> changelogEntry : changelogOffsets.entrySet()) {\n   320→            final long offset = changelogEntry.getValue();\n   321→\n   322→            if (offset != OffsetCheckpoint.OFFSET_UNKNOWN) {\n   323→                if (offset < 0) {\n   324→                    throw new StreamsException(\n   325→                            new IllegalStateException(\"Expected not to get a sentinel offset, but got: \" + changelogEntry),\n   326→                            taskId);\n   327→                }\n   328→                offsetSum += offset;\n   329→                if (offsetSum < 0) {\n   330→                    log.warn(\"Sum of changelog offsets for task {} overflowed, pinning to Long.MAX_VALUE\", taskId);\n   331→                    return Long.MAX_VALUE;\n   332→                }\n   333→            }\n   334→        }\n   335→\n   336→        return offsetSum;\n   337→    }\n   338→\n   339→    public UUID initializeProcessId() {\n   340→        if (!hasPersistentStores) {\n   341→            final UUID processId = UUID.randomUUID();\n   342→            log.info(\"Created new process id: {}\", processId);\n   343→            return processId;\n   344→        }\n   345→\n   346→        if (!lockStateDirectory()) {\n   347→            log.error(\"Unable to obtain lock as state directory is already locked by another process\");\n   348→            throw new StreamsException(String.format(\"Unable to initialize state, this can happen if multiple instances of \" +\n   349→                                           \"Kafka Streams are running in the same state directory \" +\n   350→                                           \"(current state directory is [%s]\", stateDir.getAbsolutePath()));\n   351→        }\n   352→\n   353→        final File processFile = new File(stateDir, PROCESS_FILE_NAME);\n   354→        final ObjectMapper mapper = new ObjectMapper();\n   355→\n   356→        try {\n   357→            if (processFile.exists()) {\n   358→                try {\n   359→                    final StateDirectoryProcessFile processFileData = mapper.readValue(processFile, StateDirectoryProcessFile.class);\n   360→                    log.info(\"Reading UUID from process file: {}\", processFileData.processId);\n   361→                    if (processFileData.processId != null) {\n   362→                        return processFileData.processId;\n   363→                    }\n   364→                } catch (final Exception e) {\n   365→                    log.warn(\"Failed to read json process file\", e);\n   366→                }\n   367→            }\n   368→\n   369→            final StateDirectoryProcessFile processFileData = new StateDirectoryProcessFile(UUID.randomUUID());\n   370→            log.info(\"No process id found on disk, got fresh process id {}\", processFileData.processId);\n   371→\n   372→            mapper.writeValue(processFile, processFileData);\n   373→            return processFileData.processId;\n   374→        } catch (final IOException e) {\n   375→            log.error(\"Unable to read/write process file due to unexpected exception\", e);\n   376→            throw new ProcessorStateException(e);\n   377→        }\n   378→    }\n   379→\n   380→    /**\n   381→     * Get or create the directory for the provided {@link TaskId}.\n   382→     * @return directory for the {@link TaskId}\n   383→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the task directory does not exist and could not be created\n   384→     */\n   385→    public File getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(final TaskId taskId) {\n   386→        final File taskParentDir = getTaskDirectoryParentName(taskId);\n   387→        final File taskDir = new File(taskParentDir, StateManagerUtil.toTaskDirString(taskId));\n   388→        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n   389→            if (!taskDir.exists()) {\n   390→                synchronized (taskDirCreationLock) {\n   391→                    // to avoid a race condition, we need to check again if the directory does not exist:\n   392→                    // otherwise, two threads might pass the outer `if` (and enter the `then` block),\n   393→                    // one blocks on `synchronized` while the other creates the directory,\n   394→                    // and the blocking one fails when trying to create it after it's unblocked\n   395→                    if (!taskParentDir.exists() && !taskParentDir.mkdir()) {\n   396→                        throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   397→                            String.format(\"Parent [%s] of task directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\",\n   398→                                taskParentDir.getPath(), taskDir.getPath()));\n   399→                    }\n   400→                    if (!taskDir.exists() && !taskDir.mkdir()) {\n   401→                        throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   402→                            String.format(\"task directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", taskDir.getPath()));\n   403→                    }\n   404→                }\n   405→            } else if (!taskDir.isDirectory()) {\n   406→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   407→                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", taskDir.getPath()));\n   408→            }\n   409→        }\n   410→        return taskDir;\n   411→    }\n   412→\n   413→    private File getTaskDirectoryParentName(final TaskId taskId) {\n   414→        final String namedTopology = taskId.topologyName();\n   415→        if (namedTopology != null) {\n   416→            if (!hasNamedTopologies) {\n   417→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Tried to lookup taskId with named topology, but StateDirectory thinks hasNamedTopologies = false\");\n   418→            }\n   419→            return new File(stateDir, getNamedTopologyDirName(namedTopology));\n   420→        } else {\n   421→            return stateDir;\n   422→        }\n   423→    }\n   424→\n   425→    private String getNamedTopologyDirName(final String topologyName) {\n   426→        return \"__\" + topologyName + \"__\";\n   427→    }\n   428→\n   429→    /**\n   430→     * @return The File handle for the checkpoint in the given task's directory\n   431→     */\n   432→    File checkpointFileFor(final TaskId taskId) {\n   433→        return new File(getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(taskId), StateManagerUtil.CHECKPOINT_FILE_NAME);\n   434→    }\n   435→\n   436→    /**\n   437→     * Decide if the directory of the task is empty or not\n   438→     */\n   439→    boolean directoryForTaskIsEmpty(final TaskId taskId) {\n   440→        final File taskDir = getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(taskId);\n   441→\n   442→        return taskDirIsEmpty(taskDir);\n   443→    }\n   444→\n   445→    private boolean taskDirIsEmpty(final File taskDir) {\n   446→        final File[] storeDirs = taskDir.listFiles(pathname ->\n   447→                !pathname.getName().equals(CHECKPOINT_FILE_NAME));\n   448→\n   449→        boolean taskDirEmpty = true;\n   450→\n   451→        // if the task is stateless, storeDirs would be null\n   452→        if (storeDirs != null && storeDirs.length > 0) {\n   453→            for (final File file : storeDirs) {\n   454→                // We removed the task directory locking but some upgrading applications may still have old lock files on disk,\n   455→                // we just lazily delete those in this method since it's the only thing that would be affected by these\n   456→                if (file.getName().endsWith(LOCK_FILE_NAME)) {\n   457→                    if (!file.delete()) {\n   458→                        // If we hit an error deleting this just ignore it and move on, we'll retry again at some point\n   459→                        log.warn(\"Error encountered deleting lock file in {}\", taskDir);\n   460→                    }\n   461→                } else {\n   462→                    // If it's not a lock file then the directory is not empty,\n   463→                    // but finish up the loop in case there's a lock file left to delete\n   464→                    log.trace(\"TaskDir {} was not empty, found {}\", taskDir, file);\n   465→                    taskDirEmpty = false;\n   466→                }\n   467→            }\n   468→        }\n   469→        return taskDirEmpty;\n   470→    }\n   471→\n   472→    /**\n   473→     * Get or create the directory for the global stores.\n   474→     * @return directory for the global stores\n   475→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the global store directory does not exists and could not be created\n   476→     */\n   477→    File globalStateDir() {\n   478→        final File dir = new File(stateDir, \"global\");\n   479→        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n   480→            if (!dir.exists() && !dir.mkdir()) {\n   481→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   482→                    String.format(\"global state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", dir.getPath()));\n   483→            } else if (dir.exists() && !dir.isDirectory()) {\n   484→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   485→                    String.format(\"global state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", dir.getPath()));\n   486→            }\n   487→        }\n   488→        return dir;\n   489→    }\n   490→\n   491→    private String logPrefix() {\n   492→        return String.format(\"stream-thread [%s]\", Thread.currentThread().getName());\n   493→    }\n   494→\n   495→    /**\n   496→     * Get the lock for the {@link TaskId}s directory if it is available\n   497→     * @param taskId task id\n   498→     * @return true if successful\n   499→     */\n   500→    synchronized boolean lock(final TaskId taskId) {\n   501→        if (!hasPersistentStores) {\n   502→            return true;\n   503→        }\n   504→\n   505→        final Thread lockOwner = lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n   506→        if (lockOwner != null) {\n   507→            if (lockOwner.equals(Thread.currentThread())) {\n   508→                log.trace(\"{} Found cached state dir lock for task {}\", logPrefix(), taskId);\n   509→                // we already own the lock\n   510→                return true;\n   511→            } else {\n   512→                // another thread owns the lock\n   513→                return false;\n   514→            }\n   515→        } else if (!stateDir.exists()) {\n   516→            log.error(\"Tried to lock task directory for {} but the state directory does not exist\", taskId);\n   517→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"The state directory has been deleted\");\n   518→        } else {\n   519→            lockedTasksToOwner.put(taskId, Thread.currentThread());\n   520→            return true;\n   521→        }\n   522→    }\n   523→\n   524→    /**\n   525→     * Unlock the state directory for the given {@link TaskId}.\n   526→     */\n   527→    synchronized void unlock(final TaskId taskId) {\n   528→        final Thread lockOwner = lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n   529→        if (lockOwner != null && lockOwner.equals(Thread.currentThread())) {\n   530→            lockedTasksToOwner.remove(taskId);\n   531→            log.debug(\"{} Released state dir lock for task {}\", logPrefix(), taskId);\n   532→        }\n   533→    }\n   534→\n   535→    /**\n   536→     * Expose for tests.\n   537→     */\n   538→    Thread lockOwner(final TaskId taskId) {\n   539→        return lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n   540→    }\n   541→\n   542→    @Override\n   543→    public void close() {\n   544→        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n   545→            unlockStartupStores();\n   546→            taskOffsetSums.clear();\n   547→            try {\n   548→                stateDirLock.release();\n   549→                stateDirLockChannel.close();\n   550→\n   551→                stateDirLock = null;\n   552→                stateDirLockChannel = null;\n   553→            } catch (final IOException e) {\n   554→                log.error(\"Unexpected exception while unlocking the state dir\", e);\n   555→                throw new StreamsException(String.format(\"Failed to release the lock on the state directory [%s]\", stateDir.getAbsolutePath()), e);\n   556→            }\n   557→\n   558→            // all threads should be stopped and cleaned up by now, so none should remain holding a lock\n   559→            if (!lockedTasksToOwner.isEmpty()) {\n   560→                log.error(\"Some task directories still locked while closing state, this indicates unclean shutdown: {}\", lockedTasksToOwner);\n   561→            }\n   562→        }\n   563→    }\n   564→\n   565→    public synchronized void clean() {\n   566→        try {\n   567→            cleanStateAndTaskDirectoriesCalledByUser();\n   568→        } catch (final Exception e) {\n   569→            throw new StreamsException(e);\n   570→        }\n   571→\n   572→        try {\n   573→            if (stateDir.exists()) {\n   574→                Utils.delete(globalStateDir().getAbsoluteFile());\n   575→            }\n   576→        } catch (final IOException exception) {\n   577→            log.error(\n   578→                String.format(\"%s Failed to delete global state directory of %s due to an unexpected exception\",\n   579→                    logPrefix(), appId),\n   580→                exception\n   581→            );\n   582→            throw new StreamsException(exception);\n   583→        }\n   584→\n   585→        try {\n   586→            if (hasPersistentStores && stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.delete()) {\n   587→                log.warn(\n   588→                    String.format(\"%s Failed to delete state store directory of %s for it is not empty\",\n   589→                        logPrefix(), stateDir.getAbsolutePath())\n   590→                );\n   591→            }\n   592→        } catch (final SecurityException exception) {\n   593→            log.error(\n   594→                String.format(\"%s Failed to delete state store directory of %s due to an unexpected exception\",\n   595→                    logPrefix(), stateDir.getAbsolutePath()),\n   596→                exception\n   597→            );\n   598→            throw new StreamsException(exception);\n   599→        }\n   600→    }\n   601→\n   602→    /**\n   603→     * Remove the directories for any {@link TaskId}s that are no-longer\n   604→     * owned by this {@link StreamThread} and aren't locked by either\n   605→     * another process or another {@link StreamThread}\n   606→     * @param cleanupDelayMs only remove directories if they haven't been modified for at least\n   607→     *                       this amount of time (milliseconds)\n   608→     */\n   609→    public synchronized void cleanRemovedTasks(final long cleanupDelayMs) {\n   610→        try {\n   611→            cleanRemovedTasksCalledByCleanerThread(cleanupDelayMs);\n   612→        } catch (final Exception cannotHappen) {\n   613→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Should have swallowed exception.\", cannotHappen);\n   614→        }\n   615→    }\n   616→\n   617→    private void cleanRemovedTasksCalledByCleanerThread(final long cleanupDelayMs) {\n   618→        for (final TaskDirectory taskDir : listAllTaskDirectories()) {\n   619→            final String dirName = taskDir.file().getName();\n   620→            final TaskId id = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDir.namedTopology());\n   621→            if (!lockedTasksToOwner.containsKey(id)) {\n   622→                try {\n   623→                    if (lock(id)) {\n   624→                        final long now = time.milliseconds();\n   625→                        final long lastModifiedMs = taskDir.file().lastModified();\n   626→                        if (now - cleanupDelayMs > lastModifiedMs) {\n   627→                            removeTaskOffsets(id);\n   628→                            log.info(\"{} Deleting obsolete state directory {} for task {} as {}ms has elapsed (cleanup delay is {}ms).\",\n   629→                                logPrefix(), dirName, id, now - lastModifiedMs, cleanupDelayMs);\n   630→                            removeStartupState(id);\n   631→                            Utils.delete(taskDir.file());\n   632→                        }\n   633→                    }\n   634→                } catch (final IOException exception) {\n   635→                    log.warn(\n   636→                        String.format(\"%s Swallowed the following exception during deletion of obsolete state directory %s for task %s:\",\n   637→                            logPrefix(), dirName, id),\n   638→                        exception\n   639→                    );\n   640→                } finally {\n   641→                    unlock(id);\n   642→                }\n   643→            }\n   644→        }\n   645→        // Ok to ignore returned exception as it should be swallowed\n   646→        maybeCleanEmptyNamedTopologyDirs(true);\n   647→    }\n   648→\n   649→    /**\n   650→     * Cleans up any leftover named topology directories that are empty, if any exist\n   651→     * @param logExceptionAsWarn if true, an exception will be logged as a warning\n   652→     *                       if false, an exception will be logged as error\n   653→     * @return the first IOException to be encountered\n   654→     */\n   655→    private IOException maybeCleanEmptyNamedTopologyDirs(final boolean logExceptionAsWarn) {\n   656→        if (!hasNamedTopologies) {\n   657→            return null;\n   658→        }\n   659→\n   660→        final AtomicReference<IOException> firstException = new AtomicReference<>(null);\n   661→        final File[] namedTopologyDirs = stateDir.listFiles(pathname ->\n   662→                pathname.isDirectory() && NAMED_TOPOLOGY_DIR_PATH_NAME.matcher(pathname.getName()).matches()\n   663→        );\n   664→        if (namedTopologyDirs != null) {\n   665→            for (final File namedTopologyDir : namedTopologyDirs) {\n   666→                final String topologyName = parseNamedTopologyFromDirectory(namedTopologyDir.getName());\n   667→                final Set<TaskId> taskKeys = taskOffsetSums.keySet();\n   668→                taskKeys.removeIf(taskId -> taskId.topologyName().equals(topologyName));\n   669→                final File[] contents = namedTopologyDir.listFiles();\n   670→                if (contents != null && contents.length == 0) {\n   671→                    try {\n   672→                        Utils.delete(namedTopologyDir);\n   673→                    } catch (final IOException exception) {\n   674→                        if (logExceptionAsWarn) {\n   675→                            log.warn(\n   676→                                String.format(\"%sSwallowed the following exception during deletion of named topology directory %s\",\n   677→                                    logPrefix(), namedTopologyDir.getName()),\n   678→                                exception\n   679→                            );\n   680→                        } else {\n   681→                            log.error(\n   682→                                String.format(\"%s Failed to delete named topology directory %s with exception:\",\n   683→                                    logPrefix(), namedTopologyDir.getName()),\n   684→                                exception\n   685→                            );\n   686→                        }\n   687→                        firstException.compareAndSet(null, exception);\n   688→                    }\n   689→                }\n   690→            }\n   691→        }\n   692→        return firstException.get();\n   693→    }\n   694→\n   695→    /**\n   696→     * Clears out any local state found for the given NamedTopology after it was removed\n   697→     *\n   698→     * @throws StreamsException if cleanup failed\n   699→     */\n   700→    public void clearLocalStateForNamedTopology(final String topologyName) {\n   701→        final File namedTopologyDir = new File(stateDir, getNamedTopologyDirName(topologyName));\n   702→        if (!namedTopologyDir.exists() || !namedTopologyDir.isDirectory()) {\n   703→            log.debug(\"Tried to clear out the local state for NamedTopology {} but none was found\", topologyName);\n   704→        }\n   705→        try {\n   706→            final Set<TaskId> taskKeys = taskOffsetSums.keySet();\n   707→            taskKeys.removeIf(taskId -> taskId.topologyName().equals(topologyName));\n   708→            Utils.delete(namedTopologyDir);\n   709→        } catch (final IOException e) {\n   710→            log.error(\"Hit an unexpected error while clearing local state for topology \" + topologyName, e);\n   711→            throw new StreamsException(\"Unable to delete state for the named topology \" + topologyName,\n   712→                                       e, new TaskId(-1, -1, topologyName)); // use dummy taskid to report source topology for this error\n   713→        }\n   714→    }\n   715→\n   716→    private void cleanStateAndTaskDirectoriesCalledByUser() throws Exception {\n   717→        if (!lockedTasksToOwner.isEmpty()) {\n   718→            log.warn(\"Found some still-locked task directories when user requested to cleaning up the state, \"\n   719→                + \"since Streams is not running any more these will be ignored to complete the cleanup\");\n   720→        }\n   721→        taskOffsetSums.clear();\n   722→        final AtomicReference<Exception> firstException = new AtomicReference<>();\n   723→        for (final TaskDirectory taskDir : listAllTaskDirectories()) {\n   724→            final String dirName = taskDir.file().getName();\n   725→            final TaskId id = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDir.namedTopology());\n   726→            try {\n   727→                log.info(\"{} Deleting task directory {} for {} as user calling cleanup.\",\n   728→                    logPrefix(), dirName, id);\n   729→\n   730→                if (lockedTasksToOwner.containsKey(id)) {\n   731→                    log.warn(\"{} Task {} in state directory {} was still locked by {}\",\n   732→                        logPrefix(), dirName, id, lockedTasksToOwner.get(id));\n   733→                }\n   734→                Utils.delete(taskDir.file());\n   735→            } catch (final IOException exception) {\n   736→                log.error(\n   737→                    String.format(\"%s Failed to delete task directory %s for %s with exception:\",\n   738→                        logPrefix(), dirName, id),\n   739→                    exception\n   740→                );\n   741→                firstException.compareAndSet(null, exception);\n   742→            }\n   743→        }\n   744→\n   745→        firstException.compareAndSet(null, maybeCleanEmptyNamedTopologyDirs(false));\n   746→\n   747→        final Exception exception = firstException.get();\n   748→        if (exception != null) {\n   749→            throw exception;\n   750→        }\n   751→    }\n   752→\n   753→    /**\n   754→     * List all of the task directories that are non-empty\n   755→     * @return The list of all the non-empty local directories for stream tasks\n   756→     */\n   757→    List<TaskDirectory> listNonEmptyTaskDirectories() {\n   758→        return listTaskDirectories(pathname -> {\n   759→ listTaskDirectories(final FileFilter filter) {\n   776→        final List<TaskDirectory> taskDirectories = new ArrayList<>();\n   777→        if (hasPersistentStores && stateDir.exists()) {\n   778→            if (hasNamedTopologies) {\n   779→                for (final File namedTopologyDir : listNamedTopologyDirs()) {\n   780→                    final String namedTopology = null)).collect(Collectors.toList()));\n   793→                }\n   794→            }\n   795→        }\n   796→\n   797→        return taskDirectories;\n   798→    }\n   799→\n   800→    private return namedTopologyDirectories != null ? Arrays.asList(namedTopologyDirectories) : Collections.emptyList();\n   803→    }\n   804→\n   805→    private String parseNamedTopologyFromDirectory(final String dirName) {\n   806→        return dirName.substring(2, dirName.length() - 2);\n   807→    }\n   808→\n   809→    private FileLock tryLock(final FileChannel channel) throws IOException {\n   810→        try {\n   811→            return channel.tryLock();\n   812→        } catch (final OverlappingFileLockException e) {\n   813→            return null;\n   814→        }\n   815→    }\n   816→\n   817→    public static class path\n     * @param time                system timer used to execute periodic cleanup procedure\n     * @param hasPersistentStores only when the application's topology does have stores persisted on local file\n     *                            system, we would go ahead and auto-create the corresponding application / task / store\n     *                            directories whenever necessary; otherwise no directories would be created.\n     * @param hasNamedTopologies  whether this application is composed of independent named topologies\n     *\n     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the base state directory or application state directory does not exist\n     *                                 and could not be created when hasPersistentStores is enabled.\n     */\n    public StateDirectory(final StreamsConfig config, final Time time, final boolean hasPersistentStores, final boolean hasNamedTopologies) {\n        this.time = time;\n        this.hasPersistentStores = hasPersistentStores;\n        this.hasNamedTopologies = hasNamedTopologies;\n        this.appId = config.getString(StreamsConfig.APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG);\n        this.config = config;\n        final String stateDirName = config.getString(StreamsConfig.STATE_DIR_CONFIG);\n        final File baseDir = new File(stateDirName);\n        stateDir = new File(baseDir, appId);\n\n        if (this.hasPersistentStores) {\n            if (!baseDir.exists() && !baseDir.mkdirs()) {\n                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n                    String.format(\"base state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", stateDirName));\n            }\n            if (!stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.mkdir()) {\n                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", stateDir.getPath()));\n            } else if (stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.isDirectory()) {\n                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", stateDir.getPath()));\n            }\n\n            if (stateDirName.startsWith(System.getProperty(\"java.io.tmpdir\"))) {\n                log.warn(\"Using an OS temp directory in the state.dir property can cause failures with writing\" +\n                    \" the checkpoint file due to the fact that this directory can be cleared by the OS.\" +\n                    \" Resolved state.dir: [\" + stateDirName + \"]\");\n\n            }\n            // change the dir permission to \"rwxr-x---\" to avoid world readable\n            configurePermissions(baseDir);\n            configurePermissions(stateDir);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void configurePermissions(final File file) {\n        final Path path = file.toPath();\n        if (path.getFileSystem().supportedFileAttributeViews().contains(\"posix\")) {\n            final Set<PosixFilePermission> perms = EnumSet.of(\n                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_READ,\n                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_WRITE,\n                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_EXECUTE,\n                    PosixFilePermission.GROUP_READ,\n                    PosixFilePermission.GROUP_EXECUTE\n            );\n            if (config.getBoolean(StreamsConfig.ALLOW_OS_GROUP_WRITE_ACCESS_CONFIG)) {\n                perms.add(PosixFilePermission.GROUP_WRITE);\n            }\n            try {\n                Files.setPosixFilePermissions(path, perms);\n            } catch (final IOException e) {\n                log.error(\"Error changing permissions for the directory {} \", path, e);\n            }\n        } else {\n            boolean set = file.setReadable(true, true);\n            set &= file.setWritable(true, true);\n            set &= file.setExecutable(true, true);\n            if (!set) {\n                log.error(\"Failed to change permissions for the directory {}\", file);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return true if the state directory was successfully locked\n     */\n    private boolean lockStateDirectory() {\n        final File lockFile = new File(stateDir, LOCK_FILE_NAME);\n        try {\n            stateDirLockChannel = FileChannel.open(lockFile.toPath(), StandardOpenOption.CREATE, StandardOpenOption.WRITE);\n            stateDirLock = tryLock(stateDirLockChannel);\n        } catch (final IOException e) {\n            log.error(\"Unable to lock the state directory due to unexpected exception\", e);\n            throw new ProcessorStateException(String.format(\"Failed to lock the state directory [%s] during startup\",\n                stateDir.getAbsolutePath()), e);\n        }\n        return stateDirLock != null;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @throws LockException If another process already locks any of StateDirectory\n     * @throws ProcessorStateException if any of task directory does not exist and could not be created\n    */\n    public void initializeStartupStores(final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata,\n                                        final LogContext logContext,\n                                        final StreamsMetricsImpl metricsImpl) {\n        final List<TaskDirectory> nonEmptyTaskDirectories = listNonEmptyTaskDirectories();\n        if (hasPersistentStores && !nonEmptyTaskDirectories.isEmpty()) {\n            final boolean eosEnabled = StreamsConfigUtils.eosEnabled(config);\n\n            // Initialize thread-specific resources needed to open stores in the state directory\n            final String threadLogPrefix = String.format(\"[%s]\", Thread.currentThread().getName());\n            final ThreadCache dummyCache = new ThreadCache(new LogContext(threadLogPrefix), 0L, metricsImpl);\n\n            // discover all non-empty task directories in StateDirectory\n            for (final TaskDirectory taskDirectory : nonEmptyTaskDirectories) {\n                final String dirName = taskDirectory.file().getName();\n                final TaskId task = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDirectory.namedTopology());\n                final ProcessorTopology subTopology = topologyMetadata.buildSubtopology(task);\n\n                // we still check if the task's sub-topology is stateful, even though we know its directory contains state,\n                // because it's possible that the topology has changed since that data was written, and is now stateless\n                // this therefore prevents us from creating unnecessary stores just because of some left-over state\n                if (subTopology.hasStateWithChangelogs()) {\n                    final Set<TopicPartition> inputPartitions = topologyMetadata.nodeToSourceTopics(task).values().stream()\n                            .flatMap(Collection::stream)\n                            .map(t -> new TopicPartition(t, task.partition()))\n                            .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n                    // Open a temporary state manager that will open the stores inside the subtopology\n                    final ProcessorStateManager temporaryStateManager = ProcessorStateManager.createStartupTaskStateManager(\n                        task,\n                        eosEnabled,\n                        logContext,\n                        this,\n                        subTopology.storeToChangelogTopic(),\n                        inputPartitions\n                    );\n\n                    final StartupContext initContext = new StartupContext(task, config, temporaryStateManager, metricsImpl, dummyCache);\n                    try {\n                        // We only handle TaskCorruptedException at this point. Any other exception is considered fatal.\n                        StateManagerUtil.registerStateStores(log, threadLogPrefix, subTopology, temporaryStateManager, this, initContext);\n                        temporaryStateManager.checkpoint();\n                    } catch (final TaskCorruptedException tce) {\n                        // At this point, we only log a warning and continue with the startup store initialization.\n                        // The task-corrupted exception will be handled in the first Task assignment phase.\n                        log.warn(\"Failed to register startup state stores for task {}: {}\", task, tce.getMessage());\n                    } finally {\n                        // Make sure the state manager writes the local checkpoint file before closing the stores\n                        // This will be replaced in the future when removing the checkpoint file dependency.\n                        temporaryStateManager.close();\n                    }\n                    tasksInLocalState.add(task);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    public boolean hasStartupTasks() {\n        return !tasksInLocalState.isEmpty();\n    }\n\n    public synchronized boolean removeStartupState(final TaskId taskId) {\n        final boolean removed = tasksInLocalState.remove(taskId);\n        if (removed) {\n            lockedTasksToOwner.put(taskId, Thread.currentThread());\n        }\n        return removed;\n    }\n\n\n    private void unlockStartupStores() {\n        for (final TaskId task : tasksInLocalState) {\n            unlock(task);\n        }\n    }\n\n    public Map<TaskId, Long> taskOffsetSums(final Set<TaskId> tasks) {\n        return taskOffsetSums.entrySet().stream()\n                .filter(e -> tasks.contains(e.getKey()))\n                .collect(Collectors.toMap(Map.Entry::getKey, Map.Entry::getValue));\n    }\n\n    public void updateTaskOffsets(final TaskId taskId, final Map<TopicPartition, Long> changelogOffsets) {\n        if (!changelogOffsets.isEmpty()) {\n            taskOffsetSums.put(taskId, sumOfChangelogOffsets(taskId, changelogOffsets));\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void removeTaskOffsets(final TaskId taskId) {\n        taskOffsetSums.remove(taskId);\n    }\n\n    private long sumOfChangelogOffsets(final TaskId taskId, final Map<TopicPartition, Long> changelogOffsets) {\n        long offsetSum = 0L;\n        for (final Map.Entry<TopicPartition, Long> changelogEntry : changelogOffsets.entrySet()) {\n            final long offset = changelogEntry.getValue();\n\n            if (offset != OffsetCheckpoint.OFFSET_UNKNOWN) {\n                if (offset < 0) {\n                    throw new StreamsException(\n                            new IllegalStateException(\"Expected not to get a sentinel offset, but got: \" + changelogEntry),\n                            taskId);\n                }\n                offsetSum += offset;\n                if (offsetSum < 0) {\n                    log.warn(\"Sum of changelog offsets for task {} overflowed, pinning to Long.MAX_VALUE\", taskId);\n                    return Long.MAX_VALUE;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        return offsetSum;\n    }\n\n    public UUID initializeProcessId() {\n        if (!hasPersistentStores) {\n            final UUID processId = UUID.randomUUID();\n            log.info(\"Created new process id: {}\", processId);\n            return processId;\n        }\n\n        if (!lockStateDirectory()) {\n            log.error(\"Unable to obtain lock as state directory is already locked by another process\");\n            throw new StreamsException(String.format(\"Unable to initialize state, this can happen if multiple instances of \" +\n                                           \"Kafka Streams are running in the same state directory \" +\n                                           \"(current state directory is [%s]\", stateDir.getAbsolutePath()));\n        }\n\n        final File processFile = new File(stateDir, PROCESS_FILE_NAME);\n        final ObjectMapper mapper = new ObjectMapper();\n\n        try {\n            if (processFile.exists()) {\n                try {\n                    final StateDirectoryProcessFile processFileData = mapper.readValue(processFile, StateDirectoryProcessFile.class);\n                    log.info(\"Reading UUID from process file: {}\", processFileData.processId);\n                    if (processFileData.processId != null) {\n                        return processFileData.processId;\n                    }\n                } catch (final Exception e) {\n                    log.warn(\"Failed to read json process file\", e);\n                }\n            }\n\n            final StateDirectoryProcessFile processFileData = new StateDirectoryProcessFile(UUID.randomUUID());\n            log.info(\"No process id found on disk, got fresh process id {}\", processFileData.processId);\n\n            mapper.writeValue(processFile, processFileData);\n            return processFileData.processId;\n        } catch (final IOException e) {\n            log.error(\"Unable to read/write process file due to unexpected exception\", e);\n            throw new ProcessorStateException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get or create the directory for the provided {@link TaskId}.\n     * @return directory for the {@link TaskId}\n     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the task directory does not exist and could not be created\n     */\n    public File getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(final TaskId taskId) {\n        final File taskParentDir = getTaskDirectoryParentName(taskId);\n        final File taskDir = new File(taskParentDir, StateManagerUtil.toTaskDirString(taskId));\n        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n            if (!taskDir.exists()) {\n                synchronized (taskDirCreationLock) {\n                    // to avoid a race condition, we need to check again if the directory does not exist:\n                    // otherwise, two threads might pass the outer `if` (and enter the `then` block),\n                    // one blocks on `synchronized` while the other creates the directory,\n                    // and the blocking one fails when trying to create it after it's unblocked\n                    if (!taskParentDir.exists() && !taskParentDir.mkdir()) {\n                        throw new ProcessorStateException(\n                            String.format(\"Parent [%s] of task directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\",\n                                taskParentDir.getPath(), taskDir.getPath()));\n                    }\n                    if (!taskDir.exists() && !taskDir.mkdir()) {\n                        throw new ProcessorStateException(\n                            String.format(\"task directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", taskDir.getPath()));\n                    }\n                }\n            } else if (!taskDir.isDirectory()) {\n                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", taskDir.getPath()));\n            }\n        }\n        return taskDir;\n    }\n\n    private File getTaskDirectoryParentName(final TaskId taskId) {\n        final String namedTopology = taskId.topologyName();\n        if (namedTopology != null) {\n            if (!hasNamedTopologies) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Tried to lookup taskId with named topology, but StateDirectory thinks hasNamedTopologies = false\");\n            }\n            return new File(stateDir, getNamedTopologyDirName(namedTopology));\n        } else {\n            return stateDir;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private String getNamedTopologyDirName(final String topologyName) {\n        return \"__\" + topologyName + \"__\";\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return The File handle for the checkpoint in the given task's directory\n     */\n    File checkpointFileFor(final TaskId taskId) {\n        return new File(getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(taskId), StateManagerUtil.CHECKPOINT_FILE_NAME);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Decide if the directory of the task is empty or not\n     */\n    boolean directoryForTaskIsEmpty(final TaskId taskId) {\n        final File taskDir = getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(taskId);\n\n        return taskDirIsEmpty(taskDir);\n    }\n\n    private boolean taskDirIsEmpty(final File taskDir) {\n        final File[] storeDirs = taskDir.listFiles(pathname ->\n                !pathname.getName().equals(CHECKPOINT_FILE_NAME));\n\n        boolean taskDirEmpty = true;\n\n        // if the task is stateless, storeDirs would be null\n        if (storeDirs != null && storeDirs.length > 0) {\n            for (final File file : storeDirs) {\n                // We removed the task directory locking but some upgrading applications may still have old lock files on disk,\n                // we just lazily delete those in this method since it's the only thing that would be affected by these\n                if (file.getName().endsWith(LOCK_FILE_NAME)) {\n                    if (!file.delete()) {\n                        // If we hit an error deleting this just ignore it and move on, we'll retry again at some point\n                        log.warn(\"Error encountered deleting lock file in {}\", taskDir);\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    // If it's not a lock file then the directory is not empty,\n                    // but finish up the loop in case there's a lock file left to delete\n                    log.trace(\"TaskDir {} was not empty, found {}\", taskDir, file);\n                    taskDirEmpty = false;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return taskDirEmpty;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get or create the directory for the global stores.\n     * @return directory for the global stores\n     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the global store directory does not exists and could not be created\n     */\n    File globalStateDir() {\n        final File dir = new File(stateDir, \"global\");\n        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n            if (!dir.exists() && !dir.mkdir()) {\n                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n                    String.format(\"global state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", dir.getPath()));\n            } else if (dir.exists() && !dir.isDirectory()) {\n                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n                    String.format(\"global state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", dir.getPath()));\n            }\n        }\n        return dir;\n    }\n\n    private String logPrefix() {\n        return String.format(\"stream-thread [%s]\", Thread.currentThread().getName());\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get the lock for the {@link TaskId}s directory if it is available\n     * @param taskId task id\n     * @return true if successful\n     */\n    synchronized boolean lock(final TaskId taskId) {\n        if (!hasPersistentStores) {\n            return true;\n        }\n\n        final Thread lockOwner = lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n        if (lockOwner != null) {\n            if (lockOwner.equals(Thread.currentThread())) {\n                log.trace(\"{} Found cached state dir lock for task {}\", logPrefix(), taskId);\n                // we already own the lock\n                return true;\n            } else {\n                // another thread owns the lock\n                return false;\n            }\n        } else if (!stateDir.exists()) {\n            log.error(\"Tried to lock task directory for {} but the state directory does not exist\", taskId);\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"The state directory has been deleted\");\n        } else {\n            lockedTasksToOwner.put(taskId, Thread.currentThread());\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Unlock the state directory for the given {@link TaskId}.\n     */\n    synchronized void unlock(final TaskId taskId) {\n        final Thread lockOwner = lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n        if (lockOwner != null && lockOwner.equals(Thread.currentThread())) {\n            lockedTasksToOwner.remove(taskId);\n            log.debug(\"{} Released state dir lock for task {}\", logPrefix(), taskId);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Expose for tests.\n     */\n    Thread lockOwner(final TaskId taskId) {\n        return lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() {\n        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n            unlockStartupStores();\n            taskOffsetSums.clear();\n            try {\n                stateDirLock.release();\n                stateDirLockChannel.close();\n\n                stateDirLock = null;\n                stateDirLockChannel = null;\n            } catch (final IOException e) {\n                log.error(\"Unexpected exception while unlocking the state dir\", e);\n                throw new StreamsException(String.format(\"Failed to release the lock on the state directory [%s]\", stateDir.getAbsolutePath()), e);\n            }\n\n            // all threads should be stopped and cleaned up by now, so none should remain holding a lock\n            if (!lockedTasksToOwner.isEmpty()) {\n                log.error(\"Some task directories still locked while closing state, this indicates unclean shutdown: {}\", lockedTasksToOwner);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    public synchronized void clean() {\n        try {\n            cleanStateAndTaskDirectoriesCalledByUser();\n        } catch (final Exception e) {\n            throw new StreamsException(e);\n        }\n\n        try {\n            if (stateDir.exists()) {\n                Utils.delete(globalStateDir().getAbsoluteFile());\n            }\n        } catch (final IOException exception) {\n            log.error(\n                String.format(\"%s Failed to delete global state directory of %s due to an unexpected exception\",\n                    logPrefix(), appId),\n                exception\n            );\n            throw new StreamsException(exception);\n        }\n\n        try {\n            if (hasPersistentStores && stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.delete()) {\n                log.warn(\n                    String.format(\"%s Failed to delete state store directory of %s for it is not empty\",\n                        logPrefix(), stateDir.getAbsolutePath())\n                );\n            }\n        } catch (final SecurityException exception) {\n            log.error(\n                String.format(\"%s Failed to delete state store directory of %s due to an unexpected exception\",\n                    logPrefix(), stateDir.getAbsolutePath()),\n                exception\n            );\n            throw new StreamsException(exception);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Remove the directories for any {@link TaskId}s that are no-longer\n     * owned by this {@link StreamThread} and aren't locked by either\n     * another process or another {@link StreamThread}\n     * @param cleanupDelayMs only remove directories if they haven't been modified for at least\n     *                       this amount of time (milliseconds)\n     */\n    public synchronized void cleanRemovedTasks(final long cleanupDelayMs) {\n        try {\n            cleanRemovedTasksCalledByCleanerThread(cleanupDelayMs);\n        } catch (final Exception cannotHappen) {\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Should have swallowed exception.\", cannotHappen);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void cleanRemovedTasksCalledByCleanerThread(final long cleanupDelayMs) {\n        for (final TaskDirectory taskDir : listAllTaskDirectories()) {\n            final String dirName = taskDir.file().getName();\n            final TaskId id = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDir.namedTopology());\n            if (!lockedTasksToOwner.containsKey(id)) {\n                try {\n                    if (lock(id)) {\n                        final long now = time.milliseconds();\n                        final long lastModifiedMs = taskDir.file().lastModified();\n                        if (now - cleanupDelayMs > lastModifiedMs) {\n                            removeTaskOffsets(id);\n                            log.info(\"{} Deleting obsolete state directory {} for task {} as {}ms has elapsed (cleanup delay is {}ms).\",\n                                logPrefix(), dirName, id, now - lastModifiedMs, cleanupDelayMs);\n                            removeStartupState(id);\n                            Utils.delete(taskDir.file());\n                        }\n                    }\n                } catch (final IOException exception) {\n                    log.warn(\n                        String.format(\"%s Swallowed the following exception during deletion of obsolete state directory %s for task %s:\",\n                            logPrefix(), dirName, id),\n                        exception\n                    );\n                } finally {\n                    unlock(id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        // Ok to ignore returned exception as it should be swallowed\n        maybeCleanEmptyNamedTopologyDirs(true);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Cleans up any leftover named topology directories that are empty, if any exist\n     * @param logExceptionAsWarn if true, an exception will be logged as a warning\n     *                       if false, an exception will be logged as error\n     * @return the first IOException to be encountered\n     */\n    private IOException maybeCleanEmptyNamedTopologyDirs(final boolean logExceptionAsWarn) {\n        if (!hasNamedTopologies) {\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        final AtomicReference<IOException> firstException = new AtomicReference<>(null);\n        final File[] namedTopologyDirs = stateDir.listFiles(pathname ->\n                pathname.isDirectory() && NAMED_TOPOLOGY_DIR_PATH_NAME.matcher(pathname.getName()).matches()\n        );\n        if (namedTopologyDirs != null) {\n            for (final File namedTopologyDir : namedTopologyDirs) {\n                final String topologyName = parseNamedTopologyFromDirectory(namedTopologyDir.getName());\n                final Set<TaskId> taskKeys = taskOffsetSums.keySet();\n                taskKeys.removeIf(taskId -> taskId.topologyName().equals(topologyName));\n                final File[] contents = namedTopologyDir.listFiles();\n                if (contents != null && contents.length == 0) {\n                    try {\n                        Utils.delete(namedTopologyDir);\n                    } catch (final IOException exception) {\n                        if (logExceptionAsWarn) {\n                            log.warn(\n                                String.format(\"%sSwallowed the following exception during deletion of named topology directory %s\",\n                                    logPrefix(), namedTopologyDir.getName()),\n                                exception\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            log.error(\n                                String.format(\"%s Failed to delete named topology directory %s with exception:\",\n                                    logPrefix(), namedTopologyDir.getName()),\n                                exception\n                            );\n                        }\n                        firstException.compareAndSet(null, exception);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return firstException.get();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Clears out any local state found for the given NamedTopology after it was removed\n     *\n     * @throws StreamsException if cleanup failed\n     */\n    public void clearLocalStateForNamedTopology(final String topologyName) {\n        final File namedTopologyDir = new File(stateDir, getNamedTopologyDirName(topologyName));\n        if (!namedTopologyDir.exists() || !namedTopologyDir.isDirectory()) {\n            log.debug(\"Tried to clear out the local state for NamedTopology {} but none was found\", topologyName);\n        }\n        try {\n            final Set<TaskId> taskKeys = taskOffsetSums.keySet();\n            taskKeys.removeIf(taskId -> taskId.topologyName().equals(topologyName));\n            Utils.delete(namedTopologyDir);\n        } catch (final IOException e) {\n            log.error(\"Hit an unexpected error while clearing local state for topology \" + topologyName, e);\n            throw new StreamsException(\"Unable to delete state for the named topology \" + topologyName,\n                                       e, new TaskId(-1, -1, topologyName)); // use dummy taskid to report source topology for this error\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void cleanStateAndTaskDirectoriesCalledByUser() throws Exception {\n        if (!lockedTasksToOwner.isEmpty()) {\n            log.warn(\"Found some still-locked task directories when user requested to cleaning up the state, \"\n                + \"since Streams is not running any more these will be ignored to complete the cleanup\");\n        }\n        taskOffsetSums.clear();\n        final AtomicReference<Exception> firstException = new AtomicReference<>();\n        for (final TaskDirectory taskDir : listAllTaskDirectories()) {\n            final String dirName = taskDir.file().getName();\n            final TaskId id = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDir.namedTopology());\n            try {\n                log.info(\"{} Deleting task directory {} for {} as user calling cleanup.\",\n                    logPrefix(), dirName, id);\n\n                if (lockedTasksToOwner.containsKey(id)) {\n                    log.warn(\"{} Task {} in state directory {} was still locked by {}\",\n                        logPrefix(), dirName, id, lockedTasksToOwner.get(id));\n                }\n                Utils.delete(taskDir.file());\n            } catch (final IOException exception) {\n                log.error(\n                    String.format(\"%s Failed to delete task directory %s for %s with exception:\",\n                        logPrefix(), dirName, id),\n                    exception\n                );\n                firstException.compareAndSet(null, exception);\n            }\n        }\n\n        firstException.compareAndSet(null, maybeCleanEmptyNamedTopologyDirs(false));\n\n        final Exception exception = firstException.get();\n        if (exception != null) {\n            throw exception;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * List all of the task directories that are non-empty\n     * @return The list of all the non-empty local directories for stream tasks\n     */\n    List<TaskDirectory> listNonEmptyTaskDirectories() {\n        return listTaskDirectories(pathname -> {\n            if (!pathname.isDirectory() || !TASK_DIR_PATH_NAME.matcher(pathname.getName()).matches()) {\n                return false;\n            } else {\n                return !taskDirIsEmpty(pathname);\n            }\n        });\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * List all of the task directories along with their parent directory if they belong to a named topology\n     * @return The list of all the existing local directories for stream tasks\n     */\n    List<TaskDirectory> listAllTaskDirectories() {\n        return listTaskDirectories(pathname -> pathname.isDirectory() && TASK_DIR_PATH_NAME.matcher(pathname.getName()).matches());\n    }\n\n    private List<TaskDirectory> listTaskDirectories(final FileFilter filter) {\n        final List<TaskDirectory> taskDirectories = new ArrayList<>();\n        if (hasPersistentStores && stateDir.exists()) {\n            if (hasNamedTopologies) {\n                for (final File namedTopologyDir : listNamedTopologyDirs()) {\n                    final String namedTopology = parseNamedTopologyFromDirectory(namedTopologyDir.getName());\n                    final File[] taskDirs = namedTopologyDir.listFiles(filter);\n                    if (taskDirs != null) {\n                        taskDirectories.addAll(Arrays.stream(taskDirs)\n                            .map(f -> new TaskDirectory(f, namedTopology)).collect(Collectors.toList()));\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                final File[] taskDirs =\n                    stateDir.listFiles(filter);\n                if (taskDirs != null) {\n                    taskDirectories.addAll(Arrays.stream(taskDirs)\n                                               .map(f -> new TaskDirectory(f, null)).collect(Collectors.toList()));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        return taskDirectories;\n    }\n\n    private List<File> listNamedTopologyDirs() {\n        final File[] namedTopologyDirectories = stateDir.listFiles(f -> f.getName().startsWith(\"__\") &&  f.getName().endsWith(\"__\"));\n        return namedTopologyDirectories != null ? Arrays.asList(namedTopologyDirectories) : Collections.emptyList();\n    }\n\n    private String parseNamedTopologyFromDirectory(final String dirName) {\n        return dirName.substring(2, dirName.length() - 2);\n    }\n\n    private FileLock tryLock(final FileChannel channel) throws IOException {\n        try {\n            return channel.tryLock();\n        } catch (final OverlappingFileLockException e) {\n            return null;\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static class TaskDirectory {\n        private final File file;\n        private final String namedTopology; // may be null if hasNamedTopologies = false\n\n        TaskDirectory(final File file, final String namedTopology) {\n            this.file = file;\n            this.namedTopology = namedTopology;\n
