limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.tasks;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaFuture;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.internals.KafkaFutureImpl;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.LogContext;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.errors.StreamsException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.errors.TaskMigratedException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.ProcessingThread;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.ReadOnlyTask;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.StreamTask;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.Task;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.TaskExecutionMetadata;\n\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\n\nimport java.time.Duration;\nimport java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicBoolean;\nimport java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicReference;\n\nimport static org.apache.kafka.streams.internals.StreamsConfigUtils.ProcessingMode.EXACTLY_ONCE_V2;\n\npublic class DefaultTaskExecutor implements TaskExecutor {\n\n    private class TaskExecutorThread extends Thread implements ProcessingThread {\n\n        private final AtomicBoolean shutdownRequested = new AtomicBoolean(false);\n        private final AtomicReference<KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask>> taskReleaseRequested = new AtomicReference<>(null);\n\n        private final Logger log;\n\n        public TaskExecutorThread(final String name) {\n            super(name);\n            final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", name);\n            final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n            log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskExecutor.class);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void run() {\n            log.info(\"Task executor thread started\");\n            try {\n                while (!shutdownRequested.get()) {\n                    try {\n                        runOnce(time.milliseconds());\n                    } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n                        handleException(e);\n                    } catch (final Exception e) {\n                        handleException(new StreamsException(e));\n                    }\n                }\n            } finally {\n                if (currentTask != null) {\n                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} due to shutdown.\", currentTask.id());\n                    unassignCurrentTask();\n                }\n\n                final KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask> taskReleaseFuture;\n                if ((taskReleaseFuture = taskReleaseRequested.getAndSet(null)) != null) {\n                    log.debug(\"Asked to return current task, but shutting down.\");\n                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(null);\n                }\n                log.info(\"Task executor thread shutdown\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        private void handleTaskReleaseRequested() {\n            final KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask> taskReleaseFuture;\n            if ((taskReleaseFuture = taskReleaseRequested.getAndSet(null)) != null) {\n                if (currentTask != null) {\n                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} upon request.\", currentTask.id());\n                    final StreamTask unassignedTask = unassignCurrentTask();\n                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(unassignedTask);\n                } else {\n                    log.debug(\"Asked to return current task, but returned current task already.\");\n                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(null);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        private void handleException(final StreamsException e) {\n            if (currentTask != null) {\n                taskManager.setUncaughtException(e, currentTask.id());\n\n                log.debug(\"Releasing task {} due to uncaught exception.\", currentTask.id());\n                unassignCurrentTask();\n            } else {\n                // If we do not currently have a task assigned and still get an error, this is fatal for the executor thread\n                throw e;\n            }\n        }\n\n        private void runOnce(final long nowMs) {\n            handleTaskReleaseRequested();\n\n            if (currentTask == null) {\n                currentTask = taskManager.assignNextTask(DefaultTaskExecutor.this);\n            }\n\n            if (currentTask == null) {\n                try {\n                    taskManager.awaitProcessableTasks(shutdownRequested::get);\n                } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n                    // Can be ignored, the cause of the interrupted will be handled in the event loop\n                }\n            } else {\n                boolean progressed = false;\n\n                if (taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(currentTask, nowMs) && currentTask.isProcessable(nowMs)) {\n                    if (processTask(currentTask, nowMs, time)) {\n                        log.trace(\"processed a record for {}\", currentTask.id());\n                        progressed = true;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(currentTask)) {\n                    if (currentTask.maybePunctuateStreamTime()) {\n                        log.trace(\"punctuated stream time for task {} \", currentTask.id());\n                        progressed = true;\n                    }\n                    if (currentTask.maybePunctuateSystemTime()) {\n                        log.trace(\"punctuated system time for task {} \", currentTask.id());\n                        progressed = true;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (!progressed) {\n                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} because we are not making progress.\", currentTask.id());\n                    unassignCurrentTask();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        private boolean processTask(final Task task, final long now, final Time time) {\n            boolean processed = false;\n            try {\n                processed = task.process(now);\n                if (processed) {\n                    log.trace(\"Successfully processed task {}\", task.id());\n                    task.clearTaskTimeout();\n                    // TODO: enable regardless of whether using named topologies\n                    if (taskExecutionMetadata.hasNamedTopologies() && taskExecutionMetadata.processingMode() != EXACTLY_ONCE_V2) {\n                        taskExecutionMetadata.addToSuccessfullyProcessed(task);\n                    }\n                }\n            } catch (final TimeoutException timeoutException) {\n                // TODO consolidate TimeoutException retries with general error handling\n                task.maybeInitTaskTimeoutOrThrow(now, timeoutException);\n                log.error(\n                    String.format(\n                        \"Could not complete processing records for %s due to the following exception; will move to next task and retry later\",\n                        task.id()),\n                    timeoutException\n                );\n            } catch (final TaskMigratedException e) {\n                log.info(\"Failed to process stream task {} since it got migrated to another thread already. \" +\n                    \"Will trigger a new rebalance and close all tasks as zombies together.\", task.id());\n                throw e;\n            } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n                log.error(String.format(\"Failed to process stream task %s due to the following error:\", task.id()), e);\n                e.setTaskId(task.id());\n                throw e;\n            } catch (final RuntimeException e) {\n                log.error(String.format(\"Failed to process stream task %s due to the following error:\", task.id()), e);\n                throw new StreamsException(e, task.id());\n            } finally {\n                task.recordProcessBatchTime(time.milliseconds() - now);\n            }\n            return processed;\n        }\n\n        private StreamTask unassignCurrentTask() {\n            if (currentTask == null)\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Does not own any task while being ask to unassign from task manager\");\n\n            // flush the task before giving it back to task manager, if we are not handing it back because of an error.\n            if (!taskManager.hasUncaughtException(currentTask.id())) {\n                try {\n                    currentTask.flush();\n                } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n                    log.error(String.format(\"Failed to flush stream task %s due to the following error:\", currentTask.id()), e);\n                    e.setTaskId(currentTask.id());\n                    taskManager.setUncaughtException(e, currentTask.id());\n                } catch (final RuntimeException e) {\n                    log.error(String.format(\"Failed to flush stream task %s due to the following error:\", currentTask.id()), e);\n                    taskManager.setUncaughtException(new StreamsException(e, currentTask.id()), currentTask.id());\n                }\n            }\n            taskManager.unassignTask(currentTask, DefaultTaskExecutor.this);\n\n            final StreamTask retTask = currentTask;\n            currentTask = null;\n            return retTask;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private final Time time;\n    private final String name;\n    private final TaskManager taskManager;\n    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    private final Logger log;\n\n    private StreamTask currentTask = null;\n    private TaskExecutorThread taskExecutorThread = null;\n\n    public DefaultTaskExecutor(final TaskManager taskManager,\n                               final String name,\n                               final Time time,\n                               final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n        this.time = time;\n        this.name = name;\n        this.taskManager = taskManager;\n        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(name);\n        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskExecutor.class);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String name() {\n        return name;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void start() {\n        if (taskExecutorThread == null) {\n            taskExecutorThread = new TaskExecutorThread(name);\n            taskExecutorThread.start();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean isRunning() {\n        return taskExecutorThread != null && taskExecutorThread.isAlive();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void requestShutdown() {\n        if (taskExecutorThread != null) {\n            taskExecutorThread.shutdownRequested.set(true);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void awaitShutdown(final Duration timeout) {\n        if (taskExecutorThread != null) {\n            try {\n                taskExecutorThread.join(timeout.toMillis());\n                if (taskExecutorThread.isAlive()) {\n                    throw new StreamsException(\"State updater thread did not shutdown within the timeout\");\n                }\n                taskExecutorThread = null;\n            } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public ReadOnlyTask currentTask() {\n        return currentTask != null ? new ReadOnlyTask(currentTask) : null;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public KafkaFuture<StreamTask> unassign() {\n        final KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask> future = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n\n        if (taskExecutorThread != null) {\n            log.debug(\"Asking {} to hand back task\", taskExecutorThread.getName());\n            if (!taskExecutorThread.taskReleaseRequested.compareAndSet(null, future)) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"There was already a task release request registered\");\n            }\n            if (!taskExecutorThread.isAlive()) {\n                log.debug(\"Completing future, because task executor was just shut down\");\n                future.complete(null);\n            } else {\n                taskManager.signalTaskExecutors();\n            }\n        } else {\n            log.debug(\"Tried to unassign but no thread is running\");\n            future.complete(null);\n        }\n\n        return future;\n    }\n}\n","numLines":302,"startLine":1,"totalLines":302}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01MboTQwz3Wwai9bvQhtjz4V","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"`SynchronizedPartitionGroup` wraps an `AbstractPartitionGroup` with synchronized methods - so the lock is the `SynchronizedPartitionGroup` instance. Let me think about how this interacts with `Tasks` (which is also synchronized on its own instance).\n\nA task executor thread calls `task.process()` → which might use `SynchronizedPartitionGroup` → which is synchronized on the partition group. A stream thread might call `addRawRecords` on the same partition group → which is also synchronized.\n\nBut this is not a deadlock in itself - it's contention on a single lock. For a deadlock, we need two locks acquired in different orders.\n \nNow I'm wondering if the real issue is a lock ordering problem between `Tasks` and `DefaultTaskManager.tasksLock`. If `assignNextTask()` acquires `tasksLock` and then tries to access a `Tasks` instance that's already locked by another thread, while that thread is waiting for `tasksLock`, we'd have a classic deadlock scenario. .activeInitializedTasks()` which is `synchronized` on `Tasks`.\n\nSo: `DefaultTaskManager.tasksLock` → `Tasks` monitor\n\nIs there any path that goes `Tasks` monitor → `DefaultTaskManager.tasksLock`? The Tasks class methods are synchronized but they just access internal data structures. They don't call back into DefaultTaskManager. So probably not.\n\nActually wait - but what if the `StreamThread` accesses `Tasks` directly (via synchronized methods) AND also calls into `DefaultTaskManager`? The stream thread might:\n1. Hold `Tasks` lock (via a synchronized method on Tasks)\n2. Then call `DefaultTaskManager.lockTasks()` which needs `tasksLock`\n\nAnd simultaneously, a task executor thread might:\n1. Hold `DefaultTaskManager.tasksLock` (via `assignNextTask`)\n2. Then call `tasks.activeInitializedTasks()` which needs `Tasks` lock\n\nThat's a classic ABBA deadlock scenario.    final Duration timeout = Duration.ZERO;\n        // Create a mutable copy to support iterator.remove()\n        final Set<StreamTask> restoredTasks = new LinkedHashSet<>(stateUpdater.drainRestoredActiveTasks(timeout));\n        final Iterator<StreamTask> iterator = restoredTasks.iterator();\n\n        try {\n            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n                final StreamTask task = iterator.next();\n                transitRestoredTaskToRunning(task, now, offsetResetter);\n                iterator.remove(); // Remove successfully transitioned tasks\n            }\n        } finally {\n            // Add back any tasks that we drained but didn't successfully transition\n            // from the state updater, so that they are closed during shutdown.\n            for (final Task task : restoredTasks) {\n                stateUpdater.add(task);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Handle the revoked partitions and prepare for closing the associated tasks in {@link #handleAssignment(Map, Map)}\n     * We should commit the revoking tasks first before suspending them as we will not officially own them anymore when\n     * {@link #handleAssignment(Map, Map)} is called. Note that only active task partitions are passed in from the\n     * rebalance listener, so we only need to consider/commit active tasks here\n     *\n     * If eos-v2 is used, we must commit ALL tasks. task.id());\n            } else {\n                logMessage = String.format(\"Task %s can't be processed: topology is paused\", task.id());\n            }\n        } else {\n            if (pausedTopologies.contains(topologyName)) {\n                canProcess = false;\n                logMessage = String.format(\"Task %s can't be processed: topology '%s' is paused\", task.id(), topologyName);\n            } else {\n                final NamedTopologyMetadata metadata = topologyNameToErrorMetadata.get(topologyName);\n                canProcess = metadata == null || (metadata.canProcess() && metadata.canProcessTask(task, now));\n                if (canProcess) {\n                    logMessage = String.format(\"Task %s can be processed for named topology '%s'\", task.id(), topologyName);\n                } else {\n                    logMessage = String.format(\"Task %s can't be processed for named topology '%s'\", task.id(), topologyName);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        \n        if (!canProcess) {\n            if (taskWasReady) {\n                // READY -> NOT_READY - start timer\n                taskToLastNotReadyLogTime.put(task.id(), now);\n            } else {\n                // NOT_READY - check if it should log\n                maybeLogNotReady(task.id(), now, logMessage);\n now);\n                tasksToErrorTime.remove(task.id());\n                if (tasksToErrorTime.isEmpty()) {\n                    topologyNameToErrorMetadata.remove(task.id().topologyName());\n                }\n                return true;\n            } else {\n                log.debug(\"Skipping processing for unhealthy task {} at t={}\", task.id(), now);\n                return false;\n            }\n        }\n\n        public synchronized void registerTaskError(final Task task, final Throwable t, final long now) {\n            log.info(\"Begin backoff for unhealthy task {} at t={} due to This doesn't seem like a deadlock source.\n\nLet me think about this more systematically. The problem says \"cyclic lock dependency\" - which means we need at least two locks acquired in opposite order by two different threads. Let me look at the `StreamsMetricsImpl` which had 18 synchronized usages.\n\nActually, wait. Let me look at this problem from a different angle. Let me check whether the `TopologyMetricsCache` interacts with other locks.\n \nNow I'm focusing on the stream thread loop itself. The main thread executes a sequence of operations through the task manager - checking state updates, handling topology changes, polling for records, processing them, punctuating, committing, and signaling task executors. Meanwhile, the `DefaultTaskExecutor` threads are running in parallel, and there's likely a lock ordering issue between how the stream thread and executor threads access shared state. I've confirmed both lock orderings are consistent—`tasksLock` then `Tasks` lock—so there's no deadlock risk there. Now I'm checking whether the stream thread's task processing actually acquires `tasksLock` during execution, and it looks like it doesn't; the lock is only grabbed later during the commit phase. I need to verify whether `activeInitializedTasks()` returns a snapshot or a live view that could cause issues. modifiable collection that still holds the `Tasks` lock (via a synchronized view). Let me look at how this method works - it returns an unmodifiable wrapper around the live map's values, but the synchronization only covers the method itself, not the iteration that happens afterward.\n\nSo when `DefaultTaskManager.assignNextTask()` calls this method while holding `tasksLock`, then iterates over the result, I'm seeing a lock ordering of `tasksLock` → `Tasks` lock. The critical question is whether any code path does the reverse - acquiring the `Tasks` lock first and then `tasksLock`. Looking through the `Tasks` methods, they're all synchronized but don't call back into `DefaultTaskManager`, so that reverse ordering doesn't seem to exist.\n\nNow I need to dig deeper into `DefaultStateUpdater` since that's where the most complex locking happens with three different locks involved. The concern is whether `performActionsOnTasks()` holding `tasksAndActionsLock` could lead to acquiring `restoredActiveTasksLock` through `removeTask()` and `removeRestoredTask()`. I've verified the lock ordering is consistent across all threads, so that's not the issue. Let me try running a test with Fray to detect the deadlock programmatically instead of hunting through the code manually. 40→ * All tasks contained by the Streams instance.\n    41→ *\n    42→ * Note that these tasks are shared between the TaskManager (stream thread) and the StateUpdater (restore thread),\n    43→ * i.e. all running active tasks are processed by the former and all restoring active tasks and standby tasks are\n    44→ * processed by the latter.\n    45→ */\n    46→class Tasks implements TasksRegistry {\n    47→    private final Logger log;\n    48→\n    49→    // note that these two maps may be accessed by concurrent threads and hence\n    50→    // should be synchronized when accessed\n    51→    private final Map<TaskId, StreamTask> activeTasksPerId = new TreeMap<>();\n    52→    private final Map<TaskId, StandbyTask> standbyTasksPerId = new TreeMap<>();\n    53→\n    54→    // Tasks may have been assigned for a NamedTopology that is not yet known by this host. When that occurs we stash\n    55→    // these unknown tasks until either the corresponding NamedTopology is added and we can create them at last, or\n    56→    // we receive a new assignment and they are revoked from the thread.\n    57→    private final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> pendingActiveTasksToCreate = new HashMap<>();\n    58→    private final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> Tasks(final LogContext logContext) {\n    66→        this.log = logContext.logger(getClass());\n    67→    }\n    68→\n    69→    @Override\n    70→    public void clearPendingTasksToCreate() {\n    71→        pendingActiveTasksToCreate.clear();\n    72→        pendingStandbyTasksToCreate.clear();\n    73→    }\n    74→\n    75→    @Override\n    76→    public Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> drainPendingActiveTasksForTopologies(final Set<String> currentTopologies) {\n    77→        final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> pendingActiveTasksForTopologies =\n    78→            filterMap(pendingActiveTasksToCreate, t -> currentTopologies.contains(t.getKey().topologyName()));\n    79→\n    80→        pendingActiveTasksToCreate.keySet().removeAll(pendingActiveTasksForTopologies.keySet());\n    81→\n    82→        return pendingActiveTasksForTopologies;\n    83→    }\n    84→\n    85→    @Override\n    86→    public Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> drainPendingStandbyTasksForTopologies(final Set<String> currentTopologies) {\n    87→        final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> (iterator.hasNext()) {\n   117→            final Task task = iterator.next();\n   118→            if (task.isActive()) {\n   119→                result.add((StreamTask) task);\n   120→                iterator.remove();\n   121→            }\n   122→        }\n   123→        return result;\n   124→    }\n   125→\n   126→    @Override\n   127→    public Set<StandbyTask> (iterator.hasNext()) {\n   131→            final Task task = iterator.next();\n   132→            if (!task.isActive()) {\n   133→                result.add((StandbyTask) task);\n   134→        pendingTasksToInit.addAll(tasks);\n   148→    }\n   149→\n   150→    @Override\n   151→    public boolean hasPendingTasksToInit() {\n   152→        return !pendingTasksToInit.isEmpty();\n 175→            }\n   176→        }\n   177→    }\n   178→\n   179→    @Override\n   180→    public void addStandbyTasks(final Collection<StandbyTask> newTasks) {\n   181→        if (!newTasks.isEmpty()) {\n   182→            for (final StandbyTask standbyTask : newTasks) {\n   183→                addStandbyTask(standbyTask);\n   184→            }\n   185→        }\n   186→    }\n   187→\n   188→    @Override\n   189→    public void addTask(final Task task) {\n   190→        if (task.isActive()) {\n   191→            addActiveTask((StreamTask) task);\n   192→        } else {\n   193→            addStandbyTask((StandbyTask) task);\n   194→        }\n   195→    }\n   196→\n   197→    @Override\n   198→    public synchronized void addActiveTask(final StreamTask task) {\n   199→        final TaskId taskId = task.id();\n   200→\n   201→        if (activeTasksPerId.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   202→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Attempted to create an active task that we already own: \" + taskId);\n   203→        }\n   204→\n   205→        if (standbyTasksPerId.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   206→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Attempted to create an active task while we already own its standby: \" + taskId);\n   207→        }\n   208→\n   209→        activeTasksPerId.put(taskId, task);\n   210→        pendingActiveTasksToCreate.remove(taskId);\n   211→        for (final TopicPartition topicPartition : task.inputPartitions()) {\n   212→            activeTasksPerPartition.put(topicPartition, task);\n   213→        }\n   214→    }\n   215→\n   216→    @Override\n   217→    public synchronized void addStandbyTask(final StandbyTask task) {\n   218→        final TaskId taskId = task.id();\n   219→\n   220→        if (standbyTasksPerId.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   221→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Attempted to create an standby task that we already own: \" + taskId);\n   222→        }\n   223→\n   224→        if (activeTasksPerId.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   225→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Attempted to create an standby task while we already own its active: \" + taskId);\n   226→        }\n   227→\n   228→        standbyTasksPerId.put(taskId, task);\n   229→    }\n   230→\n   231→    @Override\n   232→    public void addFailedTask(final Task task) {\n   233→        failedTaskIds.add(task.id());\n   234→        addTask(task);\n   235→    }\n   236→\n   237→    @Override\n   238→    public synchronized void removeTask(final Task taskToRemove) {\n   239→        final TaskId taskId = taskToRemove.id();\n   240→\n   241→        if (taskToRemove.state() != Task.State.CLOSED && taskToRemove.state() != State.SUSPENDED) {\n   242→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Attempted to remove a task that is not closed or suspended: \" + taskId);\n   243→        }\n   244→\n   245→        if (pendingTasksToClose.contains(taskToRemove)) {\n   246→            pendingTasksToClose.remove(taskToRemove);\n   247→        } else if (taskToRemove.isActive()) {\n   248→            if (activeTasksPerId.remove(taskId) == null) {\n   249→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Attempted to remove an active task that is not owned: \" + taskId);\n   250→            }\n   251→            removePartitionsForActiveTask(taskId);\n   252→ taskId);\n   255→            }\n   256→        }\n   257→        failedTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   258→    }\n   259→\n   260→    @Override\n   261→    public synchronized void replaceStandbyWithActive(final StreamTask activeTask) {\n   262→        final TaskId taskId = activeTask.id();\n   263→        if (standbyTasksPerId.remove(taskId) == null) {\n   264→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Attempted to convert unknown standby task to stream task: \" + taskId);\n   265→        }\n   266→\n   267→        activeTasksPerId.put(activeTask.id(), activeTask);\n   268→        for (final TopicPartition topicPartition : activeTask.inputPartitions()) {\n   269→            activeTasksPerPartition.put(topicPartition, activeTask);\n   270→        }\n   271→    }\n   272→\n   273→    @Override\n   274→    public boolean updateActiveTaskInputPartitions(final StreamTask task, final Set<TopicPartition> topicPartitions) {\n   275→        final boolean requiresUpdate =     .filter(task -> !failedTaskIds.contains(task.id()))\n   371→            .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n   372→        final Set<Task> nonFailedStandbyTasks = standbyTasksPerId.values().stream()\n   373→            .filter(task -> !failedTaskIds.contains(task.id()))\n   374→            .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n   375→        return union(HashSet::new, nonFailedActiveTasks, TaskExecutorThread extends Thread implements ProcessingThread {\n    43→\n    44→        private final AtomicBoolean shutdownRequested = new AtomicBoolean(false);\n    45→        private final        log.info(\"Task executor thread started\");\n    59→            try {\n    60→                while (!shutdownRequested.get()) {\n    61→                    try {\n    62→         log.debug(\"Asked to return current task, but shutting down.\");\n    78→                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(null);\n    79→                }\n    80→                log.info(\"Task              taskReleaseFuture.complete(null);\n    94→                }\n    95→            }\n    96→        }\n    97→\n    98→        private void handleException(final StreamsException e) {\n    99→            if (currentTask != null) {\n   100→                taskManager.setUncaughtException(e, currentTask.id());\n   101→\n   102→                log.debug(\"Releasing task {} due to uncaught exception.\", currentTask.id());\n   103→                unassignCurrentTask();\n   104→            } else {\n   105→                // If we do not currently have a task assigned and still get an error, this is fatal for the executor thread\n   106→                throw e;\n   107→            }\n   108→        }\n   109→\n   110→        private void runOnce(final long nowMs) {\n   111→            handleTaskReleaseRequested();\n   112→\n   113→            if (currentTask == null) {\n   114→                currentTask = taskManager.assignNextTask(DefaultTaskExecutor.this);\n   115→            }\n   116→\n   117→            if (currentTask == null) {\n   118→                try {\n   119→                    taskManager.awaitProcessableTasks(shutdownRequested::get);\n   120→                } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n   121→                    // Can be ignored, the cause of the interrupted will be handled in the event loop\n   122→                }\n   123→            } else {\n   124→                boolean progressed = false;\n   125→\n   126→                if (taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(currentTask, nowMs) && currentTask.isProcessable(nowMs)) {\n   127→                    if (processTask(currentTask, nowMs, time)) {\n   128→                        log.trace(\"processed a record for {}\", currentTask.id());\n   129→                        progressed = true;\n   130→                    }\n   131→                }\n   132→\n   133→                if (taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(currentTask)) {\n   134→                    if (currentTask.maybePunctuateStreamTime()) {\n   135→                        log.trace(\"punctuated stream time for task {} \", currentTask.id());\n   136→                        progressed = true;\n   137→                    }\n   138→                    if (currentTask.maybePunctuateSystemTime()) {\n   139→                        log.trace(\"punctuated system time for task {} \", currentTask.id());\n   140→                        progressed = true;\n   141→                    }\n   142→                }\n   143→\n   144→                if (!progressed) {\n   145→                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} because we are not making progress.\", currentTask.id());\n   146→                    unassignCurrentTask();\n   147→                }\n   148→            }\n   149→        }\n   150→\n   151→        private boolean processTask(final Task task, final long now, final Time time) {\n   152→            boolean processed = false;\n   153→            try {\n   154→                processed = task.process(now);\n   155→                if (processed) {\n   156→                    log.trace(\"Successfully processed task {}\", task.id());\n   157→                    task.clearTaskTimeout();\n   158→                    // TODO: enable regardless of whether using named topologies\n   159→                    if (taskExecutionMetadata.hasNamedTopologies() && taskExecutionMetadata.processingMode() != EXACTLY_ONCE_V2) {\n   160→                        taskExecutionMetadata.addToSuccessfullyProcessed(task);\n   161→                    }\n   162→                }\n   163→            } catch (final TimeoutException timeoutException) {\n   164→                // TODO consolidate TimeoutException retries with general error handling\n   165→                task.maybeInitTaskTimeoutOrThrow(now, timeoutException);\n   166→                log.error(\n   167→                    String.format(\n   168→                        \"Could not complete processing records for %s due to the following exception; will move to next task and retry later\",\n   169→                        task.id()),\n   170→                    timeoutException\n   171→                \"Will trigger a new rebalance and close all tasks as zombies together.\", task.id());\n   175→                throw e;\n   176→            } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n   177→                log.error(String.format(\"Failed to process stream task %s due to the following error:\", task.id()), e);\n   178→                e.setTaskId(task.id());\n   179→                throw              throw new StreamsException(e, task.id());\n   183→            } finally {\n   184→                task.recordProcessBatchTime(time.milliseconds() - now);\n   185→            }\n   186→            return processed;\n   187→        }\n   188→\n   189→        private StreamTask unassignCurrentTask() {\n   190→            if (currentTask == null)\n   191→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Does not own any task while being ask to unassign from task manager\");\n   192→\n   193→            // flush the task before giving it back to task manager, if we are not handing it back because of an error.\n   194→            if (!taskManager.hasUncaughtException(currentTask.id())) {\n   195→                try {\n   196→                    currentTask.flush();\n   197→                } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n   198→                    log.error(String.format(\"Failed to flush stream task %s due to the following error:\", currentTask.id()), e);\n   199→                    e.setTaskId(currentTask.id());\n   200→                    taskManager.setUncaughtException(e, currentTask.id());\n   201→                } catch (final RuntimeException e) {\n   202→                    log.error(String.format(\"Failed to flush stream task %s due to the following error:\", currentTask.id()), e);\n   203→                    taskManager.setUncaughtException(new StreamsException(e, currentTask.id()), currentTask.id());\n   204→                }\n   205→            }\n   206→            taskManager.unassignTask(currentTask, DefaultTaskExecutor.this);\n   207→\n   208→            final StreamTask retTask = currentTask;\n   209→            currentTask = null;\n   210→            return retTask;\n   211→        }\n   212→    }\n   213→\n   214→    private final Time time;\n   215→    private final String name;\n   216→    private final TaskManager taskManager;\n   217→    private final TaskExecutionMetadata It's assigned to one of the executors for processing.\n    50→ * 2. It's locked for committing, removal, other manipulations etc.\n    51→ * 3. Neither 1 or 2, i.e. it stays idle. This is possible if we do not have enough executors or because those tasks\n    52→ *    are not processable (e.g. because no records fetched) yet.\n    53→ */\n    54→public final class DefaultTaskManager implements TaskManager {\n    55→\n    56→    private final Time time;\n    57→    private final Logger log;\n    58→    private final TasksRegistry tasks;\n    59→\n    60→    private final Lock tasksLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    61→    private final Condition tasksCondition = tasksLock.newCondition();\n    62→    private final List<TaskId> lockedTasks = new ArrayList<>();\n    63→    private final Map<TaskId, StreamsException> uncaughtExceptions = new HashMap<>();\n    64→    private final Map<TaskId, TaskExecutor> assignedTasks = new HashMap<>();\n    65→    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    66→\n    67→    private final List<TaskExecutor> taskExecutors;\n    68→\n    69→    public static class DefaultTaskExecutorCreator implements TaskExecutorCreator {\n    70→        @Override\n    71→        public TaskExecutor create(final TaskManager taskManager, final String name, final Time time, final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n    72→            return new DefaultTaskExecutor(taskManager, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata);\n    73→        }\n    74→    }\n    75→\n    76→    public DefaultTaskManager(final Time time,\n    77→                              final String clientId,\n    78→                              final TasksRegistry tasks,\n    79→                              final TaskExecutorCreator executorCreator,\n    80→                              final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata,\n    81→                              final int numExecutors\n    82→                              ) {\n    83→        final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", clientId);\n    84→        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n    85→        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskManager.class);\n    86→        this.time = time;\n    87→        this.tasks = tasks;\n    88→        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n    89→\n    90→        this.taskExecutors = new ArrayList<>(numExecutors);\n    91→        for (int i = 1; i <= numExecutors; i++) {\n    92→            final String name = clientId + \"-TaskExecutor-\" + i;\n    93→            this.taskExecutors.add(executorCreator.create(this, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata));\n    94→        }\n    95→    }\n    96→\n    97→    @Override\n    98→    public StreamTask assignNextTask(final TaskExecutor executor) {\n    99→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   100→            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n   101→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for getting next task to assign is unrecognized\");\n   102→            }\n   103→\n   104→            // the most naive scheduling algorithm for now: give the next unlocked, unassigned, and  processable task\n   105→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   106→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   107→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   108→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   109→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   110→                ) {\n   111→\n   112→                    assignedTasks.put(task.id(), executor);\n   113→\n   114→                    log.debug(\"Assigned task {} to executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   115→\n   116→                    return task;\n   117→                }\n   118→            }\n   119→\n   120→            log.debug(\"Found no assignable task for executor {}\", executor.name());\n   121→\n   122→            return null;\n   123→        });\n   124→    }\n   125→\n   126→    @Override\n   127→    public void awaitProcessableTasks(final Supplier<Boolean> isShuttingDown) throws InterruptedException {\n   128→        final boolean interrupted = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   129→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   130→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   131→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   132→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   133→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   134→                ) {\n   135→                    log.debug(\"Await unblocked: returning early from await since a processable task {} was found\", task.id());\n   136→                    return false;\n   137→                }\n   138→            }\n   139→            try {\n   140→                // We re-check the shutdownRequest atomic boolean to avoid a race condition. If this thread was\n   141→                // previously interrupted while awaiting tasksCondition, it is possible to miss the signalAll that\n   142→                // is called during shutdown. If this happens, we end up blocking in the await forever.\n   143→                if (!isShuttingDown.get()) {\n   144→                    log.debug(\"Await blocking\");\n   145→                    tasksCondition.await();\n   146→                } else {\n   147→                    log.debug(\"Not awaiting since shutdown was requested\");\n   148→                }\n   149→            } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n   150→                // we interrupt the thread for shut down and pause.\n   151→                // we can ignore this exception.\n   152→                log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Interrupted while waiting for processable tasks\");\n   153→                return true;\n   154→            }\n   155→            log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Woken up to check for processable tasks\");\n   156→            return false;\n   157→        });\n   158→\n   159→        if (interrupted) {\n   160→            throw new InterruptedException();\n   161→        }\n   162→    }\n   163→\n   164→    public void signalTaskExecutors() {\n   165→        log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   166→        executeWithTasksLocked(tasksCondition::signalAll);\n   167→    }\n   168→\n   169→    @Override\n   170→    public void unassignTask(final StreamTask task, final TaskExecutor executor) {\n   171→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   172→ public KafkaFuture<Void> lockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   190→        final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> result = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n   191→\n   192→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   193→            result.complete(null);\n   194→            return result;\n   195→        }\n   196→\n   197→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   198→            lockedTasks.addAll(taskIds);\n but it's not owned\");\n   207→                }\n   208→\n   209→                if (!task.isActive()) {\n   210→                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The locking task \" + taskId + \" is not an active task\");\n   211→                }\n   212→\n   213→                if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   214→                    final TaskExecutor executor = assignedTasks.get(taskId);\n   215→                    log.debug(\"Requesting release of task {} from {}\", taskId, executor.name());\n   216→                    final KafkaFuture<StreamTask> future = executor.unassign();\n   217→                    future.whenComplete((streamTask, throwable) -> {\n   218→                        if (throwable != null) {\n   219→                            result.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n   220→                        } else {\n   221→                            assert !assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId);\n   222→                            // It can happen that the executor handed back the task before we asked it to\n   223→                            // in which case `streamTask` will be null here.\n   224→ 227→                                result.complete(null);\n   228→                            }\n   229→                        }\n   230→                    });\n   231→                } else {\n   232→                    remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   233→                    if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   234→                        result.complete(null);\n   235→ );\n   248→    }\n   249→\n   250→    @Override\n   251→    public void unlockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   252→\n   253→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   254→            return;\n   255→ executeWithTasksLocked(final Runnable action) {\n   348→        tasksLock.lock();\n   349→        try {\n   350→            action.run();\n   351→        } finally {\n   352→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   353→        }\n   354→    }\n   355→\n   356→    private <T> T returnWithTasksLocked(final Supplier<T> action) {\n   357→        tasksLock.lock();\n   358→        try {\n   359→            return action.get();\n   360→        } finally {\n   361→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   362→        }\n   363→    }\n   364→\n   365→    private boolean canProgress(final 290→     * Removes the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n   291→     */\n   292→    public KafkaFuture<Void> unregisterTopology(final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> removeTopologyFuture,\n   293→                                                final String topologyName) {\n   294→        try {\n   295→            lock();\n   296→            log.info(\"Beginning removal of NamedTopology {}, old topology version is {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n   297→            version.topologyVersion.incrementAndGet();\n   298→            version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.add(new TopologyVersionListener(topologyVersion(), removeTopologyFuture));\n   299→            final InternalTopologyBuilder removedBuilder = builders.remove(topologyName);\n   300→            removedBuilder.fullSourceTopicNames().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n   301→            removedBuilder.allSourcePatternStrings().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n   302→            log.info(\"Finished removing NamedTopology {}, topology version was updated to {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n   303→        } catch (final Throwable throwable) {\n   304→            log.error(\"Failed to remove NamedTopology {}, please retry.\", topologyName);\n   305→            removeTopologyFuture.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n   306→        } finally {\n   307→            unlock();\n   308→        }\n   309→        return removeTopologyFuture;\n   310→    }\n builder.topologyConfigs().getTaskConfig();\n   315→    }\n   316→\n   317→    public void buildAndRewriteTopology() {\n   318→        applyToEachBuilder(this::buildAndVerifyTopology);\n   319→    }\n   320→\n   321→    private void buildAndVerifyTopology(final InternalTopologyBuilder builder) {\n   322→        builder.rewriteTopology(config);\n   323→        builder.buildTopology();\n   324→\n   325→        final Set<String> allInputTopicsCopy = new HashSet<>(allInputTopics);\n   326→\n   327→        // As we go, check each topology for overlap in the set of input topics/patterns\n   328→        final int numInputTopics = allInputTopicsCopy.size();\n   329→        final List<String> inputTopics = builder.fullSourceTopicNames();\n   330→        final Collection<String> inputPatterns = builder.allSourcePatternStrings();\n   331→\n   332→        final Set<String> newInputTopics = new HashSet<>(inputTopics);\n   333→        newInputTopics.addAll(inputPatterns);\n   334→\n   335→        final int numNewInputTopics = newInputTopics.size();\n   336→        allInputTopicsCopy.addAll(newInputTopics);\n   337→\n   338→        if (allInputTopicsCopy.size() != numInputTopics + numNewInputTopics) {\n   339→            inputTopics.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n   340→            inputPatterns.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n   341→            log.error(\"Tried to add the NamedTopology {} but it had overlap with other input topics {} or patterns {}\",\n   342→                      builder.topologyName(), inputTopics, inputPatterns);\n   343→            throw new TopologyException(\"Named Topologies may not subscribe to the same input topics or patterns\");\n   344→        }\n   345→\n   346→        final ProcessorTopology globalTopology = builder.buildGlobalStateTopology();\n   347→        if (globalTopology != null) {\n   348→            if (builder.topologyName() != null) {\n   349→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Global state stores are not supported with Named Topologies\");\n   350→            } else if (this.globalTopology != null) {\n   351→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology builder had global state, but global topology has already been set\");\n   352→            } else {\n   353→                this.globalTopology = globalTopology;\n   354→                globalStateStores.putAll(builder.globalStateStores());\n   355→            }\n   356→        }\n   357→        allInputTopics.addAll(newInputTopics);\n   358→    }\n   359→\n   360→    public int numStreamThreads(final StreamsConfig config) {\n   361→        final int configuredNumStreamThreads = config.getInt(StreamsConfig.NUM_STREAM_THREADS_CONFIG);\n   362→\n   363→        // If there are named topologies but some are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n   364→        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n   365→            if (hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n   366→                log.error(\"Detected a named topology with no input topics, a named topology may not be empty.\");\n   367→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n   368→                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or pattern.\");\n   369→            }\n   370→        } else {\n   371→            // If both the global and non-global topologies are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n   372→            if (hasNoLocalTopology() && !hasGlobalTopology()) {\n   373→                log.error(\"Topology with no input topics will create no stream threads and no global thread.\");\n   374→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n   375→                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or global table.\");\n   376→            }\n   377→        }\n   378→\n   379→        // Lastly we check for an empty non-global topology and override the threads to zero if set otherwise\n   380→        if (configuredNumStreamThreads != 0 && hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n   381→            log.info(\"Overriding number of StreamThreads to zero for global-only topology\");\n   382→            return 0;\n   383→        }\n   384→\n   385→        return configuredNumStreamThreads;\n   386→    }\n   387→\n   388→    /**\n   389→     * @return true iff the app is using named topologies, or was started up with no topology at all\n   390→     */\n   391→    public boolean   212→     * @throws TaskMigratedException\n   213→     */\n   214→    boolean handleCorruption(final Set<TaskId> corruptedTasks) {\n   215→        final Set<TaskId> activeTasks = new HashSet<>(tasks.activeInitializedTaskIds());\n   216→\n   217→        // We need to stop all processing, since we need to commit non-corrupted tasks as well.\n   218→        maybeLockTasks(activeTasks);\n   219→\n   220→        final Set<Task> corruptedActiveTasks = new TreeSet<>(Comparator.comparing(Task::id));\n   221→        final Set<Task> corruptedStandbyTasks = new TreeSet<>(Comparator.comparing(Task::id));\n   222→\n   223→        for (final TaskId taskId : corruptedTasks) {\n   224→            final Task task = tasks.initializedTask(taskId);\n   225→            if (task.isActive()) {\n   226→                corruptedActiveTasks.add(task);\n   227→            } else {\n   228→                corruptedStandbyTasks.add(task);\n   229→            }\n   230→        }\n   231→\n   232→        // Make sure to clean up any corrupted standby tasks in their entirety before committing\n   233→        // since TaskMigrated can be thrown and the resulting handleLostAll will only clean up active tasks\n   234→        closeDirtyAndRevive(corruptedStandbyTasks, true);\n   235→\n   236→        // We need to commit before closing the corrupted active tasks since this will force the ongoing txn to abort\n   237→        try {\n   238→            final Collection<Task> tasksToCommit = tasks.allInitializedTasksPerId()\n   239→                .values()\n   240→                .stream()\n   241→                .filter(t -> t.state() == Task.State.RUNNING)\n   242→                .filter(t -> !corruptedTasks.contains(t.id()))\n   243→                .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n   244→            commitTasksAndMaybeUpdateCommittableOffsets(tasksToCommit, new HashMap<>());\n   245→        } catch (final TaskCorruptedException e) {\n   246→            log.info(\"Some additional tasks were found corrupted while trying to commit, these will be added to the \" +\n   247→                         \"tasks to clean and revive: {}\", e.corruptedTasks());\n   248→            corruptedActiveTasks.addAll(tasks.initializedTasks(e.corruptedTasks()));\n   249→        } catch (final TimeoutException e) {\n   250→            log.info(\"Hit TimeoutException when committing all non-corrupted tasks, these will be closed and revived\");\n   251→            final Collection<Task> uncorruptedTasks = new HashSet<>(tasks.activeInitializedTasks());\n   252→            uncorruptedTasks.removeAll(corruptedActiveTasks);\n   253→            // Those tasks which just timed out can just be closed dirty without marking changelogs as corrupted\n   254→            closeDirtyAndRevive(uncorruptedTasks, false);\n   255→        }\n   256→\n   257→        closeDirtyAndRevive(corruptedActiveTasks, true);\n   258→\n   259→        maybeUnlockTasks(activeTasks);\n   260→\n   261→        return !corruptedActiveTasks.isEmpty();\n   262→    }\n   263→\n   264→    private void closeDirtyAndRevive(final Collection<Task> taskWithChangelogs, final boolean markAsCorrupted) {\n   265→        for (final Task task : taskWithChangelogs) {\n   266→            if (task.state() != State.CLOSED) {\n   267→                try {\n   268→                    // we do not need to take the returned offsets since we are not going to commit anyways;\n   269→                    // this call is only used for active tasks to flush the cache before suspending and\n   270→                    // closing the topology\n   271→                    task.prepareCommit(false);\n   272→                } catch (final RuntimeException swallow) {\n   273→                    log.warn(\"Error flushing cache for corrupted task {}. \" +\n   274→                        \"Since the task is closing dirty, the following exception is swallowed: {}\",\n   275→                        task.id(), swallow.getMessage());\n   276→                }\n   277→\n   278→                try {\n   279→                    task.suspend();\n   280→\n   281→                    // we need to enforce a checkpoint that removes the corrupted partitions\n   282→                    if (markAsCorrupted) {\n   283→                        task.postCommit(true);\n   284→                    }\n   285→                } catch (final RuntimeException swallow) {\n   286→                    log.warn(\"Error suspending corrupted task {}. \" +\n   287→                        \"Since the task is closing dirty, the following exception is swallowed: {}\",\n   288→                        task.id(), swallow.getMessage());\n   289→                }\n   290→                task.closeDirty();\n   291→            }\n   292→            // For active tasks pause their input partitions so we won't poll any more records\n   293→            // for this task until it has been re-initialized;\n   294→            // Note, closeDirty already clears the partition-group for the task.\n   295→            if (task.isActive()) {\n   296→                final Set<TopicPartition> currentAssignment = mainConsumer.assignment();\n   297→                final Set<TopicPartition> taskInputPartitions = task.inputPartitions();\n   298→ (!assignedToPauseAndReset.equals(taskInputPartitions)) {\n   301→                    log.warn(\n   302→                        \"Expected the current consumer assignment {} to contain the input partitions {}. \" +\n   303→                            \"Will proceed to recover.\",\n   304→                        currentAssignment,\n   305→                        taskInputPartitions\n   306→                    );\n   307→                }\n   308→\n   309→                task.addPartitionsForOffsetReset(assignedToPauseAndReset);\n   310→            }\n   311→\n   312→            tasks.removeTask(task);\n   313→            task.revive();\n   314→            tasks.addPendingTasksToInit(Collections.singleton(task));\n   315→        }\n   316→    }\n   317→\n   318→    private Collection<? extends Task> assignActiveTaskFromStartupState(final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> tasksToAssign) {\n   319→        if (stateDirectory.hasStartupTasks()) {\n   320→            final                final TaskId taskId = entry.getKey();\n   323→                if (stateDirectory.removeStartupState(taskId)) {\n   324→                    assignedTasks.put(taskId, entry.getValue());\n   325→                }\n   326→            }\n   327→            return activeTaskCreator.createTasks(mainConsumer, assignedTasks);\n   328→        } else {\n   329→            return Collections.emptySet();\n   330→        }\n   331→    }\n   332→\n   333→    private Collection<? extends Task> assignStartupTasks(final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> tasksToAssign) {\n   334→ StreamsException fatal error while creating / initializing the task\n   352→     *\n   353→     * public for upgrade testing only\n   354→     */\n   355→    public void handleAssignment(final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> activeTasks,\n   356→                                 final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> standbyTasks) {\n   357→        log.info(\"Handle new assignment with:\\n\" +\n   358→                     \"\\tNew active tasks: {}\\n\" +\n   359→                     \"\\tNew standby tasks: {}\\n\" +\n   360→                     \"\\tExisting active tasks: TreeSet<>(Comparator.comparing(Task::id));\n   373→\n   374→        final Set<TaskId> tasksToLock =\n   375→            tasks.allInitializedTaskIds().stream()\n   376→                .filter(x ->        // first put aside those unrecognized tasks because of unknown named-topologies\n   382→        tasks.clearPendingTasksToCreate();\n   383→        tasks.addPendingActiveTasksToCreate(pendingTasksToCreate(activeTasksToCreate));\n   384→        tasks.addPendingStandbyTasksToCreate(pendingTasksToCreate(standbyTasksToCreate));\n   385→        \n   386→        // first rectify all existing tasks:\n   387→        // 1. for tasks that are already owned, just update input partitions / resume and skip re-creating them\n   388→        // 2. for tasks that have changed active/standby status, just recycle and skip re-creating them\n   389→        // 3. otherwise, close them since they are no longer owned\n   390→        final Map<TaskId, tasksToRecycle,\n   395→            tasksToCloseClean,\n   396→            failedTasks\n   397→        );\n   398→        failedTasks.putAll(collectExceptionsAndFailedTasksFromStateUpdater());\n 403→\n   404→        failedTasks.putAll(taskCloseExceptions);\n   405→        maybeThrowTaskExceptions(failedTasks);\n   406→\n   407→        createNewTasks(activeTasksToCreate, standbyTasksToCreate);\n   408→    }\n   409→\n   410→    // Wrap and throw the exception in the following order\n   411→    // if at least one of the exception is a non-streams exception, then wrap and throw since it should be handled by thread's handler\n   412→    // if at least one of the exception is a streams exception, then directly throw since it should be handled by thread's handler\n   413→    // if at least one of the exception is a task-migrated exception, then directly throw since it indicates all tasks are lost\n   414→    // otherwise, all the exceptions are task-corrupted, then merge their tasks and throw a single one\n   415→    // TODO: move task-corrupted and task-migrated out of the public errors package since they are internal errors and always be\n   416→    //       handled by Streams library itself\n   417→    private void maybeThrowTaskExceptions(final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> taskExceptions) {\n   418→        if (!taskExceptions.isEmpty()) {\n   419→            log.error(\"Get exceptions for the following tasks: {}\", taskExceptions);\n   420→\n   421→            final Set<TaskId> aggregatedCorruptedTaskIds = new HashSet<>();\n   422→            StreamsException lastFatal = null;\n   423→            TaskMigratedException lastTaskMigrated = null;\n   424→            for (final Map.Entry<TaskId, RuntimeException> entry : taskExceptions.entrySet()) {\n   425→                final TaskId taskId = entry.getKey();\n   426→                final RuntimeException exception = entry.getValue();\n   427→\n   428→                if (exception instanceof StreamsException) {\n   429→                    if (exception instanceof TaskMigratedException) {\n   430→                        lastTaskMigrated = (TaskMigratedException) exception;\n   431→                    } else if (exception instanceof TaskCorruptedException) {\n   432→                        log.warn(\"Encounter corrupted task \" + taskId + \", will group it with other corrupted tasks \" +\n   433→                            \"and handle together\", exception);\n   434→                        aggregatedCorruptedTaskIds.add(taskId);\n   435→                    } else {\n   436→                        ((StreamsException) exception).setTaskId(taskId);\n   437→                        lastFatal = (StreamsException) exception;\n   438→                    }\n   439→                } else if (exception instanceof KafkaException) {\n   440→                    lastFatal = new StreamsException(exception, taskId);\n   441→                } else {\n   442→                    lastFatal = new StreamsException(\"Encounter unexpected fatal error for task \" + taskId, exception, taskId);\n   443→                }\n   444→            }\n   445→\n   446→            if (lastFatal != null) {\n   447→                throw lastFatal;\n   448→            } else if (lastTaskMigrated != null) {\n   449→                throw lastTaskMigrated;\n   450→            } else {\n   451→                throw new TaskCorruptedException(aggregatedCorruptedTaskIds);\n   452→            }\n   453→        }\n   454→    }\n   455→\n   456→    private void createNewTasks(final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> activeTasksToCreate,\n   457→                                final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> standbyTasksToCreate) {\n   458→        final Collection<StreamTask> newActiveTasks = activeTaskCreator.createTasks(mainConsumer, activeTasksToCreate);\n   459→        final Collection<StandbyTask> newStandbyTasks = standbyTaskCreator.createTasks(standbyTasksToCreate);\n   460→\n   461→        tasks.addPendingTasksToInit(newActiveTasks);\n   462→        tasks.addPendingTasksToInit(newStandbyTasks);\n   463→    }\n   464→\n   465→    private void updateInputPartitionsOfStandbyTaskIfTheyChanged(final Task task,\n   466→                                                                 final Set<TopicPartition> inputPartitions) {\n   467→        /*\n   468→        We should only update input partitions of a standby task if the input partitions really changed. Updating the\n   469→        input partitions of tasks also updates the mapping from source nodes to input topics in the processor topology\n   470→        within the task. The mapping is updated with the topics from the topology metadata. The topology metadata does\n   471→        not prefix intermediate internal topics with the application ID. Thus, if a standby task has input partitions\n   472→        from an intermediate internal topic the update of the mapping in the processor topology leads to an invalid\n   473→        topology exception during recycling of a standby task to an active task when the input queues are created. This\n   474→        is because the input topics in the processor topology and the input partitions of the task do not match because\n   475→        the former miss the application ID prefix.\n   476→        For standby task that have only input partitions from intermediate internal topics this check avoids the invalid\n   477→        topology exception. Unfortunately, a subtopology might have input partitions subscribed to with a regex\n   478→        additionally intermediate internal topics which might still lead to an invalid topology exception during recycling\n   479→        irrespectively of this check here. Thus, there is still a bug to fix here.\n   480→         */\n   481→        if (!task.inputPartitions().equals(inputPartitions)) {\n   482→            task.updateInputPartitions(inputPartitions, topologyMetadata.nodeToSourceTopics(task.id()));\n   483→        }\n   484→    }\n   485→\n   486→    private void handleTasks(final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> activeTasksToCreate,\n   487→                             final Map<TaskId, Set<TopicPartition>> standbyTasksToCreate,\n   488→                             final Map<Task, Set<TopicPartition>> tasksToRecycle,\n   489→                             final Set<Task> tasksToCloseClean,\n   490→                             final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> failedTasks) {\n   491→        handleTasksPendingInitialization();\n   492→        handleExistingStateForTasks(activeTasksToCreate, standbyTasksToCreate);\n   493→        handleRestoringAndUpdatingTasks(activeTasksToCreate, standbyTasksToCreate, failedTasks);\n   494→        handleRunningAndSuspendedTasks(activeTasksToCreate, standbyTasksToCreate, tasksToRecycle, tasksToCloseClean);\n   495→    }\n   496→\n   497→    private void handleTasksPendingInitialization() {\n   498→        // All tasks pending initialization are not part of the usual bookkeeping\n   499→\n   500→        final Set<Task> tasksToCloseDirty = new TreeSet<>(Comparator.comparing(Task::id));"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Cxq7vCw5FiCVgg4ycQCobR","session_id":"57f8f286-c279-42ec-9968-88e72246e88d","uuid":"13303e77-9cba-464e-98bf-a97a6c34db58"}

during rebalancing\n * the shard may change, which triggers listener notifications.\n */\npublic class MetricsSessionManager {\n\n    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MetricsSessionManager.class);\n\n    private final ReentrantLock sessionLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    private final Condition sessionReady = sessionLock.newCondition();\n    private final long sessionTtlMs;\n\n    private volatile MetricsSession currentSession;\n    private boolean rebalancingMode = false;\n\n    private final List<MetricsSessionListener> listeners = new CopyOnWriteArrayList<>();\n\n    public MetricsSessionManager(final long sessionTtlMs) {\n        this.sessionTtlMs = sessionTtlMs;\n        this.currentSession = new MetricsSession(\n            \"init-\" + ThreadLocalRandom.current().nextInt(10000),\n            0,\n            System.currentTimeMillis() + sessionTtlMs\n        );\n    }\n\n    public void registerListener(final MetricsSessionListener listener) {\n        listeners.add(listener);\n    }\n\n    public void setRebalancingMode(final boolean rebalancing) {\n        sessionLock.lock();\n        try {\n            this.rebalancingMode = rebalancing;\n            log.debug(\"Metrics session manager rebalancing mode set to {}\", rebalancing);\n        } finally {\n            sessionLock.unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the current session. The returned reference is safe to read\n     * without holding the lock due to the volatile field, but the session\n     * may expire at any time.\n     */\n    public MetricsSession currentSession() {\n        return currentSession;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * If the current session has expired, renew it.\n     */\n    public void refreshSessionIfExpired() {\n        sessionLock.lock();\n        try {\n            if (currentSession != null && currentSession.expiryMs > System.currentTimeMillis()) {\n                return; // still valid\n            }\n            renewSessionInternal();\n        } finally {\n            sessionLock.unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Force a session renewal regardless of expiry status.\n     */\n    public void renewSession() {\n        sessionLock.lock();\n        try {\n            renewSessionInternal();\n        } finally {\n            sessionLock.unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void renewSessionInternal() {\n        final MetricsSession oldSession = currentSession;\n        final int newShardId;\n        if (rebalancingMode) {\n            // During rebalancing, the shard assignment may change\n            newShardId = ThreadLocalRandom.current().nextInt(1024);\n        } else {\n            newShardId = oldSession != null ? oldSession.shardId : 0;\n        }\n\n        final MetricsSession newSession = new MetricsSession(\n            \"sess-\" + ThreadLocalRandom.current().nextInt(100000),\n            newShardId,\n            System.currentTimeMillis() + sessionTtlMs\n        );\n        currentSession = newSession;\n        log.debug(\"Renewed metrics session: old={}, new={}\", oldSession, newSession);\n\n        notifyListeners(oldSession, newSession);\n        sessionReady.signalAll();\n    }\n\n    private void notifyListeners(final MetricsSession oldSession, final MetricsSession newSession) {\n        for (final MetricsSessionListener listener : listeners) {\n            try {\n                listener.onSessionChanged(oldSession, newSession);\n            } catch (final Exception e) {\n                log.warn(\"Metrics session listener threw exception\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Represents an authenticated metrics session with a token, shard\n     * assignment, and expiry timestamp.\n     */\n    public static class MetricsSession {\n        public final String token;\n        public final int shardId;\n        public final long expiryMs;\n\n        public MetricsSession(final String token, final int shardId, final long expiryMs) {\n            this.token = token;\n            this.shardId = shardId;\n            this.expiryMs = expiryMs;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"MetricsSession{token='\" + token + \"', shard=\" + shardId + \", expiry=\" + expiryMs + \"}\";\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":158,"startLine":1,"totalLines":158}}}

Provides read/write locking\n * around transport lifecycle operations. Read (shared) access is used for\n * normal metric fetches; write (exclusive) access is required for reconnects\n * and maintenance operations.\n */\npublic abstract class AbstractMetricsTransport {\n\n    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AbstractMetricsTransport.class);\n\n    private final ReentrantReadWriteLock transportLock = new ReentrantReadWriteLock();\n\n    // Timestamp of last successful connect — read under lock for staleness checks\n    volatile long lastConnectTimeMs = -1L;\n\n    protected void acquireExclusive() {\n        transportLock.writeLock().lock();\n    }\n\n    protected void releaseExclusive() {\n        transportLock.writeLock().unlock();\n    }\n\n    protected void acquireShared() {\n        transportLock.readLock().lock();\n    }\n\n    protected void releaseShared() {\n        transportLock.readLock().unlock();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Establish or re-establish the underlying transport connection.\n     */\n    protected abstract void doConnect();\n\n    /**\n     * Tear down the underlying transport connection.\n     */\n    protected abstract void doDisconnect();\n\n    /**\n     * @return {@code true} if the current transport connection is still usable\n     */\n    protected abstract boolean isTransportValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Performs a full reconnect cycle under exclusive lock. path: transport is healthy, fetch under read lock\n                topologyFetchTimestamps.computeIfAbsent(topologyName, k -> System.currentTimeMillis());\n                return refreshTransport(topologyName);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Refreshes the transport connection and then fetches metrics.\n     */\n    private Map<String, Double> refreshTransport(final String validateSession();\n        } finally {\n            releaseExclusive();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void validateSession() {\n        final MetricsSessionManager.MetricsSession session = sessionManager.currentSession();\n        if (session == null || session.expiryMs <= System.currentTimeMillis()) {\n            log.debug(\"Current metrics session expired, requesting refresh\");\n metrics backend\n        topologyFetchTimestamps.put(topologyName, System.currentTimeMillis());\n        return Collections.emptyMap();\n    }\n\n    public void markTransportInvalid() {\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals;\n\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\nimport org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n\nimport java.util.HashMap;\nimport java.util.Map;\n\n/**\n * Thread-safe cache for per-topology metric snapshots. All public methods\n * are synchronized on this instance's intrinsic monitor. Cache entries\n * are evicted when they exceed the configured TTL, or when explicitly\n * invalidated (e.g. after a session shard change during rebalancing).\n */\npublic class TopologyMetricsCache {\n\n    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(TopologyMetricsCache.class);\n\n    private final Map<String, CachedMetricsSnapshot> cache = new HashMap<>();\n    private final StreamsMetricsConnector metricsConnector;\n    private final long cacheTtlMs;\n\n    public TopologyMetricsCache(final StreamsMetricsConnector metricsConnector, final long cacheTtlMs) {\n        this.metricsConnector = metricsConnector;\n        this.cacheTtlMs = cacheTtlMs;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns a cached metrics snapshot for the given topology, refreshing\n     * from the backend if the cache entry is missing or stale.\n     * Returns null if the topology is unknown.\n     */\n    public synchronized CachedMetricsSnapshot getSnapshot(final String topologyName) {\n        final CachedMetricsSnapshot cached = cache.get(topologyName);\n        if (cached != null && !cached.isExpired(cacheTtlMs)) {\n            return cached;\n        }\n        return refreshFromBackend(topologyName);\n    }\n\n    private CachedMetricsSnapshot refreshFromBackend(final String topologyName) {\n        final Map<String, Double> metrics = metricsConnector.fetchLatestMetrics(topologyName);\n        final CachedMetricsSnapshot snapshot = new CachedMetricsSnapshot(topologyName, metrics, System.currentTimeMillis());\n        cache.put(topologyName, snapshot);\n        return snapshot;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Invalidates all cached entries. Called when the metrics session's shard\n     * assignment changes, since metrics may now come from a different shard.\n     */\n    public synchronized void invalidateAll() {\n        log.debug(\"Invalidating all {} cached topology metrics snapshots\", cache.size());\n        cache.clear();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Invalidates a single topology's cached entry.\n     */\n    public synchronized void invalidateTopology(final String topologyName) {\n        cache.remove(topologyName);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return the number of currently cached snapshots\n     */\n    public synchronized int size() {\n        return cache.size();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Creates a {@link MetricsSessionListener} that invalidates this cache\n     * when the session's shard assignment changes. The listener is a no-op\n     * if the shard stays the same (which is the common case outside of\n     * rebalancing).\n     */\n    public MetricsSessionListener createInvalidationListener() {\n        return new TopologyMetricsCacheInvalidator();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Inner listener that bridges session-change events to cache invalidation.\n     * The conditional shard check means this only fires during rebalancing\n     * when the shard actually changes.\n     */\n    private class TopologyMetricsCacheInvalidator implements MetricsSessionListener {\n        @Override\n        public void onSessionChanged(final MetricsSessionManager.MetricsSession oldSession,\n                                     final MetricsSessionManager.MetricsSession newSession) {\n            if (oldSession != null && newSession != null && oldSession.shardId != newSession.shardId) {\n                log.debug(\"Shard assignment changed from {} to {}, invalidating metrics cache\",\n                    oldSession.shardId, newSession.shardId);\n                invalidateAll();\n            } else {\n                log.trace(\"Session renewed without shard change, cache remains valid\");\n topologyName;\n        private final Map<String, Double> metrics;\n        private final long fetchedAtMs;\n\n        public CachedMetricsSnapshot(final String topologyName, final Map<String, Double>
